src/backend/access/heap/heapam.c
1/*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
2 *
3 * heapam.c
4 *	  heap access method code
5 *
6 * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2025, PostgreSQL Global Development Group
7 * Portions Copyright (c) 1994, Regents of the University of California
8 *
9 *
10 * IDENTIFICATION
11 *	  src/backend/access/heap/heapam.c
12 *
13 *
14 * INTERFACE ROUTINES
15 *		heap_beginscan	- begin relation scan
16 *		heap_rescan		- restart a relation scan
17 *		heap_endscan	- end relation scan
18 *		heap_getnext	- retrieve next tuple in scan
19 *		heap_fetch		- retrieve tuple with given tid
20 *		heap_insert		- insert tuple into a relation
21 *		heap_multi_insert - insert multiple tuples into a relation
22 *		heap_delete		- delete a tuple from a relation
23 *		heap_update		- replace a tuple in a relation with another tuple
24 *
25 * NOTES
26 *	  This file contains the heap_ routines which implement
27 *	  the POSTGRES heap access method used for all POSTGRES
28 *	  relations.
29 *
30 *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
31 */

112
113/*
114 * Each tuple lock mode has a corresponding heavyweight lock, and one or two
115 * corresponding MultiXactStatuses (one to merely lock tuples, another one to
116 * update them).  This table (and the macros below) helps us determine the
117 * heavyweight lock mode and MultiXactStatus values to use for any particular
118 * tuple lock strength.
119 *
120 * These interact with InplaceUpdateTupleLock, an alias for ExclusiveLock.
121 *
122 * Don't look at lockstatus/updstatus directly!  Use get_mxact_status_for_lock
123 * instead.
124 */
125static const struct
126{
127	LOCKMODE	hwlock;
128	int			lockstatus;
129	int			updstatus;
130}
131
132			tupleLockExtraInfo[MaxLockTupleMode + 1] =
133{
134	{							/* LockTupleKeyShare */
135		AccessShareLock,
136		MultiXactStatusForKeyShare,
137		-1						/* KeyShare does not allow updating tuples */
138	},
139	{							/* LockTupleShare */
140		RowShareLock,
141		MultiXactStatusForShare,
142		-1						/* Share does not allow updating tuples */
143	},
144	{							/* LockTupleNoKeyExclusive */
145		ExclusiveLock,
146		MultiXactStatusForNoKeyUpdate,
147		MultiXactStatusNoKeyUpdate
148	},
149	{							/* LockTupleExclusive */
150		AccessExclusiveLock,
151		MultiXactStatusForUpdate,
152		MultiXactStatusUpdate
153	}
154};
155
156/* Get the LOCKMODE for a given MultiXactStatus */
157#define LOCKMODE_from_mxstatus(status) \
158			(tupleLockExtraInfo[TUPLOCK_from_mxstatus((status))].hwlock)
159
160/*
161 * Acquire heavyweight locks on tuples, using a LockTupleMode strength value.
162 * This is more readable than having every caller translate it to lock.h's
163 * LOCKMODE.
164 */
165#define LockTupleTuplock(rel, tup, mode) \
166	LockTuple((rel), (tup), tupleLockExtraInfo[mode].hwlock)
167#define UnlockTupleTuplock(rel, tup, mode) \
168	UnlockTuple((rel), (tup), tupleLockExtraInfo[mode].hwlock)
169#define ConditionalLockTupleTuplock(rel, tup, mode, log) \
170	ConditionalLockTuple((rel), (tup), tupleLockExtraInfo[mode].hwlock, (log))
171
172#ifdef USE_PREFETCH
173/*
174 * heap_index_delete_tuples and index_delete_prefetch_buffer use this
175 * structure to coordinate prefetching activity
176 */
177typedef struct
178{
179	BlockNumber cur_hblkno;
180	int			next_item;
181	int			ndeltids;
182	TM_IndexDelete *deltids;
183} IndexDeletePrefetchState;
184#endif
185
186/* heap_index_delete_tuples bottom-up index deletion costing constants */
187#define BOTTOMUP_MAX_NBLOCKS			6
188#define BOTTOMUP_TOLERANCE_NBLOCKS		3
189
190/*
191 * heap_index_delete_tuples uses this when determining which heap blocks it
192 * must visit to help its bottom-up index deletion caller
193 */
194typedef struct IndexDeleteCounts
195{
196	int16		npromisingtids; /* Number of "promising" TIDs in group */
197	int16		ntids;			/* Number of TIDs in group */
198	int16		ifirsttid;		/* Offset to group's first deltid */
199} IndexDeleteCounts;
200
201/*
202 * This table maps tuple lock strength values for each particular
203 * MultiXactStatus value.
204 */
205static const int MultiXactStatusLock[MaxMultiXactStatus + 1] =
206{
207	LockTupleKeyShare,			/* ForKeyShare */
208	LockTupleShare,				/* ForShare */
209	LockTupleNoKeyExclusive,	/* ForNoKeyUpdate */
210	LockTupleExclusive,			/* ForUpdate */
211	LockTupleNoKeyExclusive,	/* NoKeyUpdate */
212	LockTupleExclusive			/* Update */
213};
214
215/* Get the LockTupleMode for a given MultiXactStatus */
216#define TUPLOCK_from_mxstatus(status) \
217			(MultiXactStatusLock[(status)])
218
219/*
220 * Check that we have a valid snapshot if we might need TOAST access.
221 */
222static inline void
223AssertHasSnapshotForToast(Relation rel)
224{
225#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
226
227	/* bootstrap mode in particular breaks this rule */
228	if (!IsNormalProcessingMode())
229		return;
230
231	/* if the relation doesn't have a TOAST table, we are good */
232	if (!OidIsValid(rel->rd_rel->reltoastrelid))
233		return;
234
235	Assert(HaveRegisteredOrActiveSnapshot());
236
237#endif							/* USE_ASSERT_CHECKING */
238}
239
240/* ----------------------------------------------------------------
241 *						 heap support routines
242 * ----------------------------------------------------------------
243 */
244
245/*
246 * Streaming read API callback for parallel sequential scans. Returns the next
247 * block the caller wants from the read stream or InvalidBlockNumber when done.
248 */
249static BlockNumber
250heap_scan_stream_read_next_parallel(ReadStream *stream,
251									void *callback_private_data,
252									void *per_buffer_data)
253{
254	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) callback_private_data;
255
256	Assert(ScanDirectionIsForward(scan->rs_dir));
257	Assert(scan->rs_base.rs_parallel);
258
259	if (unlikely(!scan->rs_inited))
260	{
261		/* parallel scan */
262		table_block_parallelscan_startblock_init(scan->rs_base.rs_rd,
263												 scan->rs_parallelworkerdata,
264												 (ParallelBlockTableScanDesc) scan->rs_base.rs_parallel);
265
266		/* may return InvalidBlockNumber if there are no more blocks */
267		scan->rs_prefetch_block = table_block_parallelscan_nextpage(scan->rs_base.rs_rd,
268																	scan->rs_parallelworkerdata,
269																	(ParallelBlockTableScanDesc) scan->rs_base.rs_parallel);
270		scan->rs_inited = true;
271	}
272	else
273	{
274		scan->rs_prefetch_block = table_block_parallelscan_nextpage(scan->rs_base.rs_rd,
275																	scan->rs_parallelworkerdata, (ParallelBlockTableScanDesc)
276																	scan->rs_base.rs_parallel);
277	}
278
279	return scan->rs_prefetch_block;
280}
281
282/*
283 * Streaming read API callback for serial sequential and TID range scans.
284 * Returns the next block the caller wants from the read stream or
285 * InvalidBlockNumber when done.
286 */
287static BlockNumber
288heap_scan_stream_read_next_serial(ReadStream *stream,
289								  void *callback_private_data,
290								  void *per_buffer_data)
291{
292	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) callback_private_data;
293
294	if (unlikely(!scan->rs_inited))
295	{
296		scan->rs_prefetch_block = heapgettup_initial_block(scan, scan->rs_dir);
297		scan->rs_inited = true;
298	}
299	else
300		scan->rs_prefetch_block = heapgettup_advance_block(scan,
301														   scan->rs_prefetch_block,
302														   scan->rs_dir);
303
304	return scan->rs_prefetch_block;
305}
306
307/*
308 * Read stream API callback for bitmap heap scans.
309 * Returns the next block the caller wants from the read stream or
310 * InvalidBlockNumber when done.
311 */
312static BlockNumber
313bitmapheap_stream_read_next(ReadStream *pgsr, void *private_data,
314							void *per_buffer_data)
315{
316	TBMIterateResult *tbmres = per_buffer_data;
317	BitmapHeapScanDesc bscan = (BitmapHeapScanDesc) private_data;
318	HeapScanDesc hscan = (HeapScanDesc) bscan;
319	TableScanDesc sscan = &hscan->rs_base;
320
321	for (;;)
322	{
323		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
324
325		/* no more entries in the bitmap */
326		if (!tbm_iterate(&sscan->st.rs_tbmiterator, tbmres))
327			return InvalidBlockNumber;
328
329		/*
330		 * Ignore any claimed entries past what we think is the end of the
331		 * relation. It may have been extended after the start of our scan (we
332		 * only hold an AccessShareLock, and it could be inserts from this
333		 * backend).  We don't take this optimization in SERIALIZABLE
334		 * isolation though, as we need to examine all invisible tuples
335		 * reachable by the index.
336		 */
337		if (!IsolationIsSerializable() &&
338			tbmres->blockno >= hscan->rs_nblocks)
339			continue;
340
341		return tbmres->blockno;
342	}
343
344	/* not reachable */
345	Assert(false);
346}
347
348/* ----------------
349 *		initscan - scan code common to heap_beginscan and heap_rescan
350 * ----------------
351 */
352static void
353initscan(HeapScanDesc scan, ScanKey key, bool keep_startblock)
354{
355	ParallelBlockTableScanDesc bpscan = NULL;
356	bool		allow_strat;
357	bool		allow_sync;
358
359	/*
360	 * Determine the number of blocks we have to scan.
361	 *
362	 * It is sufficient to do this once at scan start, since any tuples added
363	 * while the scan is in progress will be invisible to my snapshot anyway.
364	 * (That is not true when using a non-MVCC snapshot.  However, we couldn't
365	 * guarantee to return tuples added after scan start anyway, since they
366	 * might go into pages we already scanned.  To guarantee consistent
367	 * results for a non-MVCC snapshot, the caller must hold some higher-level
368	 * lock that ensures the interesting tuple(s) won't change.)
369	 */
370	if (scan->rs_base.rs_parallel != NULL)
371	{
372		bpscan = (ParallelBlockTableScanDesc) scan->rs_base.rs_parallel;
373		scan->rs_nblocks = bpscan->phs_nblocks;
374	}
375	else
376		scan->rs_nblocks = RelationGetNumberOfBlocks(scan->rs_base.rs_rd);
377
378	/*
379	 * If the table is large relative to NBuffers, use a bulk-read access
380	 * strategy and enable synchronized scanning (see syncscan.c).  Although
381	 * the thresholds for these features could be different, we make them the
382	 * same so that there are only two behaviors to tune rather than four.
383	 * (However, some callers need to be able to disable one or both of these
384	 * behaviors, independently of the size of the table; also there is a GUC
385	 * variable that can disable synchronized scanning.)
386	 *
387	 * Note that table_block_parallelscan_initialize has a very similar test;
388	 * if you change this, consider changing that one, too.
389	 */
390	if (!RelationUsesLocalBuffers(scan->rs_base.rs_rd) &&
391		scan->rs_nblocks > NBuffers / 4)
392	{
393		allow_strat = (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_ALLOW_STRAT) != 0;
394		allow_sync = (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_ALLOW_SYNC) != 0;
395	}
396	else
397		allow_strat = allow_sync = false;
398
399	if (allow_strat)
400	{
401		/* During a rescan, keep the previous strategy object. */
402		if (scan->rs_strategy == NULL)
403			scan->rs_strategy = GetAccessStrategy(BAS_BULKREAD);
404	}
405	else
406	{
407		if (scan->rs_strategy != NULL)
408			FreeAccessStrategy(scan->rs_strategy);
409		scan->rs_strategy = NULL;
410	}
411
412	if (scan->rs_base.rs_parallel != NULL)
413	{
414		/* For parallel scan, believe whatever ParallelTableScanDesc says. */
415		if (scan->rs_base.rs_parallel->phs_syncscan)
416			scan->rs_base.rs_flags |= SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
417		else
418			scan->rs_base.rs_flags &= ~SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
419	}
420	else if (keep_startblock)
421	{
422		/*
423		 * When rescanning, we want to keep the previous startblock setting,
424		 * so that rewinding a cursor doesn't generate surprising results.
425		 * Reset the active syncscan setting, though.
426		 */
427		if (allow_sync && synchronize_seqscans)
428			scan->rs_base.rs_flags |= SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
429		else
430			scan->rs_base.rs_flags &= ~SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
431	}
432	else if (allow_sync && synchronize_seqscans)
433	{
434		scan->rs_base.rs_flags |= SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
435		scan->rs_startblock = ss_get_location(scan->rs_base.rs_rd, scan->rs_nblocks);
436	}
437	else
438	{
439		scan->rs_base.rs_flags &= ~SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
440		scan->rs_startblock = 0;
441	}
442
443	scan->rs_numblocks = InvalidBlockNumber;
444	scan->rs_inited = false;
445	scan->rs_ctup.t_data = NULL;
446	ItemPointerSetInvalid(&scan->rs_ctup.t_self);
447	scan->rs_cbuf = InvalidBuffer;
448	scan->rs_cblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
449	scan->rs_ntuples = 0;
450	scan->rs_cindex = 0;
451
452	/*
453	 * Initialize to ForwardScanDirection because it is most common and
454	 * because heap scans go forward before going backward (e.g. CURSORs).
455	 */
456	scan->rs_dir = ForwardScanDirection;
457	scan->rs_prefetch_block = InvalidBlockNumber;
458
459	/* page-at-a-time fields are always invalid when not rs_inited */
460
461	/*
462	 * copy the scan key, if appropriate
463	 */
464	if (key != NULL && scan->rs_base.rs_nkeys > 0)
465		memcpy(scan->rs_base.rs_key, key, scan->rs_base.rs_nkeys * sizeof(ScanKeyData));
466
467	/*
468	 * Currently, we only have a stats counter for sequential heap scans (but
469	 * e.g for bitmap scans the underlying bitmap index scans will be counted,
470	 * and for sample scans we update stats for tuple fetches).
471	 */
472	if (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_TYPE_SEQSCAN)
473		pgstat_count_heap_scan(scan->rs_base.rs_rd);
474}
475
476/*
477 * heap_setscanlimits - restrict range of a heapscan
478 *
479 * startBlk is the page to start at
480 * numBlks is number of pages to scan (InvalidBlockNumber means "all")
481 */
482void
483heap_setscanlimits(TableScanDesc sscan, BlockNumber startBlk, BlockNumber numBlks)
484{
485	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) sscan;
486
487	Assert(!scan->rs_inited);	/* else too late to change */
488	/* else rs_startblock is significant */
489	Assert(!(scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_ALLOW_SYNC));
490
491	/* Check startBlk is valid (but allow case of zero blocks...) */
492	Assert(startBlk == 0 || startBlk < scan->rs_nblocks);
493
494	scan->rs_startblock = startBlk;
495	scan->rs_numblocks = numBlks;
496}
497
498/*
499 * Per-tuple loop for heap_prepare_pagescan(). Pulled out so it can be called
500 * multiple times, with constant arguments for all_visible,
501 * check_serializable.
502 */
503pg_attribute_always_inline
504static int
505page_collect_tuples(HeapScanDesc scan, Snapshot snapshot,
506					Page page, Buffer buffer,
507					BlockNumber block, int lines,
508					bool all_visible, bool check_serializable)
509{
510	int			ntup = 0;
511	OffsetNumber lineoff;
512
513	for (lineoff = FirstOffsetNumber; lineoff <= lines; lineoff++)
514	{
515		ItemId		lpp = PageGetItemId(page, lineoff);
516		HeapTupleData loctup;
517		bool		valid;
518
519		if (!ItemIdIsNormal(lpp))
520			continue;
521
522		loctup.t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lpp);
523		loctup.t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lpp);
524		loctup.t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(scan->rs_base.rs_rd);
525		ItemPointerSet(&(loctup.t_self), block, lineoff);
526
527		if (all_visible)
528			valid = true;
529		else
530			valid = HeapTupleSatisfiesVisibility(&loctup, snapshot, buffer);
531
532		if (check_serializable)
533			HeapCheckForSerializableConflictOut(valid, scan->rs_base.rs_rd,
534												&loctup, buffer, snapshot);
535
536		if (valid)
537		{
538			scan->rs_vistuples[ntup] = lineoff;
539			ntup++;
540		}
541	}
542
543	Assert(ntup <= MaxHeapTuplesPerPage);
544
545	return ntup;
546}
547
548/*
549 * heap_prepare_pagescan - Prepare current scan page to be scanned in pagemode
550 *
551 * Preparation currently consists of 1. prune the scan's rs_cbuf page, and 2.
552 * fill the rs_vistuples[] array with the OffsetNumbers of visible tuples.
553 */
554void
555heap_prepare_pagescan(TableScanDesc sscan)
556{
557	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) sscan;
558	Buffer		buffer = scan->rs_cbuf;
559	BlockNumber block = scan->rs_cblock;
560	Snapshot	snapshot;
561	Page		page;
562	int			lines;
563	bool		all_visible;
564	bool		check_serializable;
565
566	Assert(BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer) == block);
567
568	/* ensure we're not accidentally being used when not in pagemode */
569	Assert(scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_ALLOW_PAGEMODE);
570	snapshot = scan->rs_base.rs_snapshot;
571
572	/*
573	 * Prune and repair fragmentation for the whole page, if possible.
574	 */
575	heap_page_prune_opt(scan->rs_base.rs_rd, buffer);
576
577	/*
578	 * We must hold share lock on the buffer content while examining tuple
579	 * visibility.  Afterwards, however, the tuples we have found to be
580	 * visible are guaranteed good as long as we hold the buffer pin.
581	 */
582	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE);
583
584	page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
585	lines = PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page);
586
587	/*
588	 * If the all-visible flag indicates that all tuples on the page are
589	 * visible to everyone, we can skip the per-tuple visibility tests.
590	 *
591	 * Note: In hot standby, a tuple that's already visible to all
592	 * transactions on the primary might still be invisible to a read-only
593	 * transaction in the standby. We partly handle this problem by tracking
594	 * the minimum xmin of visible tuples as the cut-off XID while marking a
595	 * page all-visible on the primary and WAL log that along with the
596	 * visibility map SET operation. In hot standby, we wait for (or abort)
597	 * all transactions that can potentially may not see one or more tuples on
598	 * the page. That's how index-only scans work fine in hot standby. A
599	 * crucial difference between index-only scans and heap scans is that the
600	 * index-only scan completely relies on the visibility map where as heap
601	 * scan looks at the page-level PD_ALL_VISIBLE flag. We are not sure if
602	 * the page-level flag can be trusted in the same way, because it might
603	 * get propagated somehow without being explicitly WAL-logged, e.g. via a
604	 * full page write. Until we can prove that beyond doubt, let's check each
605	 * tuple for visibility the hard way.
606	 */
607	all_visible = PageIsAllVisible(page) && !snapshot->takenDuringRecovery;
608	check_serializable =
609		CheckForSerializableConflictOutNeeded(scan->rs_base.rs_rd, snapshot);
610
611	/*
612	 * We call page_collect_tuples() with constant arguments, to get the
613	 * compiler to constant fold the constant arguments. Separate calls with
614	 * constant arguments, rather than variables, are needed on several
615	 * compilers to actually perform constant folding.
616	 */
617	if (likely(all_visible))
618	{
619		if (likely(!check_serializable))
620			scan->rs_ntuples = page_collect_tuples(scan, snapshot, page, buffer,
621												   block, lines, true, false);
622		else
623			scan->rs_ntuples = page_collect_tuples(scan, snapshot, page, buffer,
624												   block, lines, true, true);
625	}
626	else
627	{
628		if (likely(!check_serializable))
629			scan->rs_ntuples = page_collect_tuples(scan, snapshot, page, buffer,
630												   block, lines, false, false);
631		else
632			scan->rs_ntuples = page_collect_tuples(scan, snapshot, page, buffer,
633												   block, lines, false, true);
634	}
635
636	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
637}
638
639/*
640 * heap_fetch_next_buffer - read and pin the next block from MAIN_FORKNUM.
641 *
642 * Read the next block of the scan relation from the read stream and save it
643 * in the scan descriptor.  It is already pinned.
644 */
645static inline void
646heap_fetch_next_buffer(HeapScanDesc scan, ScanDirection dir)
647{
648	Assert(scan->rs_read_stream);
649
650	/* release previous scan buffer, if any */
651	if (BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))
652	{
653		ReleaseBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf);
654		scan->rs_cbuf = InvalidBuffer;
655	}
656
657	/*
658	 * Be sure to check for interrupts at least once per page.  Checks at
659	 * higher code levels won't be able to stop a seqscan that encounters many
660	 * pages' worth of consecutive dead tuples.
661	 */
662	CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
663
664	/*
665	 * If the scan direction is changing, reset the prefetch block to the
666	 * current block. Otherwise, we will incorrectly prefetch the blocks
667	 * between the prefetch block and the current block again before
668	 * prefetching blocks in the new, correct scan direction.
669	 */
670	if (unlikely(scan->rs_dir != dir))
671	{
672		scan->rs_prefetch_block = scan->rs_cblock;
673		read_stream_reset(scan->rs_read_stream);
674	}
675
676	scan->rs_dir = dir;
677
678	scan->rs_cbuf = read_stream_next_buffer(scan->rs_read_stream, NULL);
679	if (BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))
680		scan->rs_cblock = BufferGetBlockNumber(scan->rs_cbuf);
681}
682
683/*
684 * heapgettup_initial_block - return the first BlockNumber to scan
685 *
686 * Returns InvalidBlockNumber when there are no blocks to scan.  This can
687 * occur with empty tables and in parallel scans when parallel workers get all
688 * of the pages before we can get a chance to get our first page.
689 */
690static pg_noinline BlockNumber
691heapgettup_initial_block(HeapScanDesc scan, ScanDirection dir)
692{
693	Assert(!scan->rs_inited);
694	Assert(scan->rs_base.rs_parallel == NULL);
695
696	/* When there are no pages to scan, return InvalidBlockNumber */
697	if (scan->rs_nblocks == 0 || scan->rs_numblocks == 0)
698		return InvalidBlockNumber;
699
700	if (ScanDirectionIsForward(dir))
701	{
702		return scan->rs_startblock;
703	}
704	else
705	{
706		/*
707		 * Disable reporting to syncscan logic in a backwards scan; it's not
708		 * very likely anyone else is doing the same thing at the same time,
709		 * and much more likely that we'll just bollix things for forward
710		 * scanners.
711		 */
712		scan->rs_base.rs_flags &= ~SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
713
714		/*
715		 * Start from last page of the scan.  Ensure we take into account
716		 * rs_numblocks if it's been adjusted by heap_setscanlimits().
717		 */
718		if (scan->rs_numblocks != InvalidBlockNumber)
719			return (scan->rs_startblock + scan->rs_numblocks - 1) % scan->rs_nblocks;
720
721		if (scan->rs_startblock > 0)
722			return scan->rs_startblock - 1;
723
724		return scan->rs_nblocks - 1;
725	}
726}
727
728
729/*
730 * heapgettup_start_page - helper function for heapgettup()
731 *
732 * Return the next page to scan based on the scan->rs_cbuf and set *linesleft
733 * to the number of tuples on this page.  Also set *lineoff to the first
734 * offset to scan with forward scans getting the first offset and backward
735 * getting the final offset on the page.
736 */
737static Page
738heapgettup_start_page(HeapScanDesc scan, ScanDirection dir, int *linesleft,
739					  OffsetNumber *lineoff)
740{
741	Page		page;
742
743	Assert(scan->rs_inited);
744	Assert(BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf));
745
746	/* Caller is responsible for ensuring buffer is locked if needed */
747	page = BufferGetPage(scan->rs_cbuf);
748
749	*linesleft = PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) - FirstOffsetNumber + 1;
750
751	if (ScanDirectionIsForward(dir))
752		*lineoff = FirstOffsetNumber;
753	else
754		*lineoff = (OffsetNumber) (*linesleft);
755
756	/* lineoff now references the physically previous or next tid */
757	return page;
758}
759
760
761/*
762 * heapgettup_continue_page - helper function for heapgettup()
763 *
764 * Return the next page to scan based on the scan->rs_cbuf and set *linesleft
765 * to the number of tuples left to scan on this page.  Also set *lineoff to
766 * the next offset to scan according to the ScanDirection in 'dir'.
767 */
768static inline Page
769heapgettup_continue_page(HeapScanDesc scan, ScanDirection dir, int *linesleft,
770						 OffsetNumber *lineoff)
771{
772	Page		page;
773
774	Assert(scan->rs_inited);
775	Assert(BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf));
776
777	/* Caller is responsible for ensuring buffer is locked if needed */
778	page = BufferGetPage(scan->rs_cbuf);
779
780	if (ScanDirectionIsForward(dir))
781	{
782		*lineoff = OffsetNumberNext(scan->rs_coffset);
783		*linesleft = PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) - (*lineoff) + 1;
784	}
785	else
786	{
787		/*
788		 * The previous returned tuple may have been vacuumed since the
789		 * previous scan when we use a non-MVCC snapshot, so we must
790		 * re-establish the lineoff <= PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) invariant
791		 */
792		*lineoff = Min(PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page), OffsetNumberPrev(scan->rs_coffset));
793		*linesleft = *lineoff;
794	}
795
796	/* lineoff now references the physically previous or next tid */
797	return page;
798}
799
800/*
801 * heapgettup_advance_block - helper for heap_fetch_next_buffer()
802 *
803 * Given the current block number, the scan direction, and various information
804 * contained in the scan descriptor, calculate the BlockNumber to scan next
805 * and return it.  If there are no further blocks to scan, return
806 * InvalidBlockNumber to indicate this fact to the caller.
807 *
808 * This should not be called to determine the initial block number -- only for
809 * subsequent blocks.
810 *
811 * This also adjusts rs_numblocks when a limit has been imposed by
812 * heap_setscanlimits().
813 */
814static inline BlockNumber
815heapgettup_advance_block(HeapScanDesc scan, BlockNumber block, ScanDirection dir)
816{
817	Assert(scan->rs_base.rs_parallel == NULL);
818
819	if (likely(ScanDirectionIsForward(dir)))
820	{
821		block++;
822
823		/* wrap back to the start of the heap */
824		if (block >= scan->rs_nblocks)
825			block = 0;
826
827		/*
828		 * Report our new scan position for synchronization purposes. We don't
829		 * do that when moving backwards, however. That would just mess up any
830		 * other forward-moving scanners.
831		 *
832		 * Note: we do this before checking for end of scan so that the final
833		 * state of the position hint is back at the start of the rel.  That's
834		 * not strictly necessary, but otherwise when you run the same query
835		 * multiple times the starting position would shift a little bit
836		 * backwards on every invocation, which is confusing. We don't
837		 * guarantee any specific ordering in general, though.
838		 */
839		if (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_ALLOW_SYNC)
840			ss_report_location(scan->rs_base.rs_rd, block);
841
842		/* we're done if we're back at where we started */
843		if (block == scan->rs_startblock)
844			return InvalidBlockNumber;
845
846		/* check if the limit imposed by heap_setscanlimits() is met */
847		if (scan->rs_numblocks != InvalidBlockNumber)
848		{
849			if (--scan->rs_numblocks == 0)
850				return InvalidBlockNumber;
851		}
852
853		return block;
854	}
855	else
856	{
857		/* we're done if the last block is the start position */
858		if (block == scan->rs_startblock)
859			return InvalidBlockNumber;
860
861		/* check if the limit imposed by heap_setscanlimits() is met */
862		if (scan->rs_numblocks != InvalidBlockNumber)
863		{
864			if (--scan->rs_numblocks == 0)
865				return InvalidBlockNumber;
866		}
867
868		/* wrap to the end of the heap when the last page was page 0 */
869		if (block == 0)
870			block = scan->rs_nblocks;
871
872		block--;
873
874		return block;
875	}
876}
877
878/* ----------------
879 *		heapgettup - fetch next heap tuple
880 *
881 *		Initialize the scan if not already done; then advance to the next
882 *		tuple as indicated by "dir"; return the next tuple in scan->rs_ctup,
883 *		or set scan->rs_ctup.t_data = NULL if no more tuples.
884 *
885 * Note: the reason nkeys/key are passed separately, even though they are
886 * kept in the scan descriptor, is that the caller may not want us to check
887 * the scankeys.
888 *
889 * Note: when we fall off the end of the scan in either direction, we
890 * reset rs_inited.  This means that a further request with the same
891 * scan direction will restart the scan, which is a bit odd, but a
892 * request with the opposite scan direction will start a fresh scan
893 * in the proper direction.  The latter is required behavior for cursors,
894 * while the former case is generally undefined behavior in Postgres
895 * so we don't care too much.
896 * ----------------
897 */
898static void
899heapgettup(HeapScanDesc scan,
900		   ScanDirection dir,
901		   int nkeys,
902		   ScanKey key)
903{
904	HeapTuple	tuple = &(scan->rs_ctup);
905	Page		page;
906	OffsetNumber lineoff;
907	int			linesleft;
908
909	if (likely(scan->rs_inited))
910	{
911		/* continue from previously returned page/tuple */
912		LockBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf, BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE);
913		page = heapgettup_continue_page(scan, dir, &linesleft, &lineoff);
914		goto continue_page;
915	}
916
917	/*
918	 * advance the scan until we find a qualifying tuple or run out of stuff
919	 * to scan
920	 */
921	while (true)
922	{
923		heap_fetch_next_buffer(scan, dir);
924
925		/* did we run out of blocks to scan? */
926		if (!BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))
927			break;
928
929		Assert(BufferGetBlockNumber(scan->rs_cbuf) == scan->rs_cblock);
930
931		LockBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf, BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE);
932		page = heapgettup_start_page(scan, dir, &linesleft, &lineoff);
933continue_page:
934
935		/*
936		 * Only continue scanning the page while we have lines left.
937		 *
938		 * Note that this protects us from accessing line pointers past
939		 * PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(); both for forward scans when we resume the
940		 * table scan, and for when we start scanning a new page.
941		 */
942		for (; linesleft > 0; linesleft--, lineoff += dir)
943		{
944			bool		visible;
945			ItemId		lpp = PageGetItemId(page, lineoff);
946
947			if (!ItemIdIsNormal(lpp))
948				continue;
949
950			tuple->t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lpp);
951			tuple->t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lpp);
952			ItemPointerSet(&(tuple->t_self), scan->rs_cblock, lineoff);
953
954			visible = HeapTupleSatisfiesVisibility(tuple,
955												   scan->rs_base.rs_snapshot,
956												   scan->rs_cbuf);
957
958			HeapCheckForSerializableConflictOut(visible, scan->rs_base.rs_rd,
959												tuple, scan->rs_cbuf,
960												scan->rs_base.rs_snapshot);
961
962			/* skip tuples not visible to this snapshot */
963			if (!visible)
964				continue;
965
966			/* skip any tuples that don't match the scan key */
967			if (key != NULL &&
968				!HeapKeyTest(tuple, RelationGetDescr(scan->rs_base.rs_rd),
969							 nkeys, key))
970				continue;
971
972			LockBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
973			scan->rs_coffset = lineoff;
974			return;
975		}
976
977		/*
978		 * if we get here, it means we've exhausted the items on this page and
979		 * it's time to move to the next.
980		 */
981		LockBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
982	}
983
984	/* end of scan */
985	if (BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))
986		ReleaseBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf);
987
988	scan->rs_cbuf = InvalidBuffer;
989	scan->rs_cblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
990	scan->rs_prefetch_block = InvalidBlockNumber;
991	tuple->t_data = NULL;
992	scan->rs_inited = false;
993}
994
995/* ----------------
996 *		heapgettup_pagemode - fetch next heap tuple in page-at-a-time mode
997 *
998 *		Same API as heapgettup, but used in page-at-a-time mode
999 *
1000 * The internal logic is much the same as heapgettup's too, but there are some
1001 * differences: we do not take the buffer content lock (that only needs to
1002 * happen inside heap_prepare_pagescan), and we iterate through just the
1003 * tuples listed in rs_vistuples[] rather than all tuples on the page.  Notice
1004 * that lineindex is 0-based, where the corresponding loop variable lineoff in
1005 * heapgettup is 1-based.
1006 * ----------------
1007 */
1008static void
1009heapgettup_pagemode(HeapScanDesc scan,
1010					ScanDirection dir,
1011					int nkeys,
1012					ScanKey key)
1013{
1014	HeapTuple	tuple = &(scan->rs_ctup);
1015	Page		page;
1016	uint32		lineindex;
1017	uint32		linesleft;
1018
1019	if (likely(scan->rs_inited))
1020	{
1021		/* continue from previously returned page/tuple */
1022		page = BufferGetPage(scan->rs_cbuf);
1023
1024		lineindex = scan->rs_cindex + dir;
1025		if (ScanDirectionIsForward(dir))
1026			linesleft = scan->rs_ntuples - lineindex;
1027		else
1028			linesleft = scan->rs_cindex;
1029		/* lineindex now references the next or previous visible tid */
1030
1031		goto continue_page;
1032	}
1033
1034	/*
1035	 * advance the scan until we find a qualifying tuple or run out of stuff
1036	 * to scan
1037	 */
1038	while (true)
1039	{
1040		heap_fetch_next_buffer(scan, dir);
1041
1042		/* did we run out of blocks to scan? */
1043		if (!BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))
1044			break;
1045
1046		Assert(BufferGetBlockNumber(scan->rs_cbuf) == scan->rs_cblock);
1047
1048		/* prune the page and determine visible tuple offsets */
1049		heap_prepare_pagescan((TableScanDesc) scan);
1050		page = BufferGetPage(scan->rs_cbuf);
1051		linesleft = scan->rs_ntuples;
1052		lineindex = ScanDirectionIsForward(dir) ? 0 : linesleft - 1;
1053
1054		/* block is the same for all tuples, set it once outside the loop */
1055		ItemPointerSetBlockNumber(&tuple->t_self, scan->rs_cblock);
1056
1057		/* lineindex now references the next or previous visible tid */
1058continue_page:
1059
1060		for (; linesleft > 0; linesleft--, lineindex += dir)
1061		{
1062			ItemId		lpp;
1063			OffsetNumber lineoff;
1064
1065			Assert(lineindex < scan->rs_ntuples);
1066			lineoff = scan->rs_vistuples[lineindex];
1067			lpp = PageGetItemId(page, lineoff);
1068			Assert(ItemIdIsNormal(lpp));
1069
1070			tuple->t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lpp);
1071			tuple->t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lpp);
1072			ItemPointerSetOffsetNumber(&tuple->t_self, lineoff);
1073
1074			/* skip any tuples that don't match the scan key */
1075			if (key != NULL &&
1076				!HeapKeyTest(tuple, RelationGetDescr(scan->rs_base.rs_rd),
1077							 nkeys, key))
1078				continue;
1079
1080			scan->rs_cindex = lineindex;
1081			return;
1082		}
1083	}
1084
1085	/* end of scan */
1086	if (BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))
1087		ReleaseBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf);
1088	scan->rs_cbuf = InvalidBuffer;
1089	scan->rs_cblock = InvalidBlockNumber;
1090	scan->rs_prefetch_block = InvalidBlockNumber;
1091	tuple->t_data = NULL;
1092	scan->rs_inited = false;
1093}
1094
1095
1096/* ----------------------------------------------------------------
1097 *					 heap access method interface
1098 * ----------------------------------------------------------------
1099 */
1100
1101
1102TableScanDesc
1103heap_beginscan(Relation relation, Snapshot snapshot,
1104			   int nkeys, ScanKey key,
1105			   ParallelTableScanDesc parallel_scan,
1106			   uint32 flags)
1107{
1108	HeapScanDesc scan;
1109
1110	/*
1111	 * increment relation ref count while scanning relation
1112	 *
1113	 * This is just to make really sure the relcache entry won't go away while
1114	 * the scan has a pointer to it.  Caller should be holding the rel open
1115	 * anyway, so this is redundant in all normal scenarios...
1116	 */
1117	RelationIncrementReferenceCount(relation);
1118
1119	/*
1120	 * allocate and initialize scan descriptor
1121	 */
1122	if (flags & SO_TYPE_BITMAPSCAN)
1123	{
1124		BitmapHeapScanDesc bscan = palloc(sizeof(BitmapHeapScanDescData));
1125
1126		/*
1127		 * Bitmap Heap scans do not have any fields that a normal Heap Scan
1128		 * does not have, so no special initializations required here.
1129		 */
1130		scan = (HeapScanDesc) bscan;
1131	}
1132	else
1133		scan = (HeapScanDesc) palloc(sizeof(HeapScanDescData));
1134
1135	scan->rs_base.rs_rd = relation;
1136	scan->rs_base.rs_snapshot = snapshot;
1137	scan->rs_base.rs_nkeys = nkeys;
1138	scan->rs_base.rs_flags = flags;
1139	scan->rs_base.rs_parallel = parallel_scan;
1140	scan->rs_strategy = NULL;	/* set in initscan */
1141	scan->rs_cbuf = InvalidBuffer;
1142
1143	/*
1144	 * Disable page-at-a-time mode if it's not a MVCC-safe snapshot.
1145	 */
1146	if (!(snapshot && IsMVCCSnapshot(snapshot)))
1147		scan->rs_base.rs_flags &= ~SO_ALLOW_PAGEMODE;
1148
1149	/*
1150	 * For seqscan and sample scans in a serializable transaction, acquire a
1151	 * predicate lock on the entire relation. This is required not only to
1152	 * lock all the matching tuples, but also to conflict with new insertions
1153	 * into the table. In an indexscan, we take page locks on the index pages
1154	 * covering the range specified in the scan qual, but in a heap scan there
1155	 * is nothing more fine-grained to lock. A bitmap scan is a different
1156	 * story, there we have already scanned the index and locked the index
1157	 * pages covering the predicate. But in that case we still have to lock
1158	 * any matching heap tuples. For sample scan we could optimize the locking
1159	 * to be at least page-level granularity, but we'd need to add per-tuple
1160	 * locking for that.
1161	 */
1162	if (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & (SO_TYPE_SEQSCAN | SO_TYPE_SAMPLESCAN))
1163	{
1164		/*
1165		 * Ensure a missing snapshot is noticed reliably, even if the
1166		 * isolation mode means predicate locking isn't performed (and
1167		 * therefore the snapshot isn't used here).
1168		 */
1169		Assert(snapshot);
1170		PredicateLockRelation(relation, snapshot);
1171	}
1172
1173	/* we only need to set this up once */
1174	scan->rs_ctup.t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
1175
1176	/*
1177	 * Allocate memory to keep track of page allocation for parallel workers
1178	 * when doing a parallel scan.
1179	 */
1180	if (parallel_scan != NULL)
1181		scan->rs_parallelworkerdata = palloc(sizeof(ParallelBlockTableScanWorkerData));
1182	else
1183		scan->rs_parallelworkerdata = NULL;
1184
1185	/*
1186	 * we do this here instead of in initscan() because heap_rescan also calls
1187	 * initscan() and we don't want to allocate memory again
1188	 */
1189	if (nkeys > 0)
1190		scan->rs_base.rs_key = (ScanKey) palloc(sizeof(ScanKeyData) * nkeys);
1191	else
1192		scan->rs_base.rs_key = NULL;
1193
1194	initscan(scan, key, false);
1195
1196	scan->rs_read_stream = NULL;
1197
1198	/*
1199	 * Set up a read stream for sequential scans and TID range scans. This
1200	 * should be done after initscan() because initscan() allocates the
1201	 * BufferAccessStrategy object passed to the read stream API.
1202	 */
1203	if (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_TYPE_SEQSCAN ||
1204		scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_TYPE_TIDRANGESCAN)
1205	{
1206		ReadStreamBlockNumberCB cb;
1207
1208		if (scan->rs_base.rs_parallel)
1209			cb = heap_scan_stream_read_next_parallel;
1210		else
1211			cb = heap_scan_stream_read_next_serial;
1212
1213		/* ---
1214		 * It is safe to use batchmode as the only locks taken by `cb`
1215		 * are never taken while waiting for IO:
1216		 * - SyncScanLock is used in the non-parallel case
1217		 * - in the parallel case, only spinlocks and atomics are used
1218		 * ---
1219		 */
1220		scan->rs_read_stream = read_stream_begin_relation(READ_STREAM_SEQUENTIAL |
1221														  READ_STREAM_USE_BATCHING,
1222														  scan->rs_strategy,
1223														  scan->rs_base.rs_rd,
1224														  MAIN_FORKNUM,
1225														  cb,
1226														  scan,
1227														  0);
1228	}
1229	else if (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_TYPE_BITMAPSCAN)
1230	{
1231		scan->rs_read_stream = read_stream_begin_relation(READ_STREAM_DEFAULT |
1232														  READ_STREAM_USE_BATCHING,
1233														  scan->rs_strategy,
1234														  scan->rs_base.rs_rd,
1235														  MAIN_FORKNUM,
1236														  bitmapheap_stream_read_next,
1237														  scan,
1238														  sizeof(TBMIterateResult));
1239	}
1240
1241
1242	return (TableScanDesc) scan;
1243}
1244
1245void
1246heap_rescan(TableScanDesc sscan, ScanKey key, bool set_params,
1247			bool allow_strat, bool allow_sync, bool allow_pagemode)
1248{
1249	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) sscan;
1250
1251	if (set_params)
1252	{
1253		if (allow_strat)
1254			scan->rs_base.rs_flags |= SO_ALLOW_STRAT;
1255		else
1256			scan->rs_base.rs_flags &= ~SO_ALLOW_STRAT;
1257
1258		if (allow_sync)
1259			scan->rs_base.rs_flags |= SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
1260		else
1261			scan->rs_base.rs_flags &= ~SO_ALLOW_SYNC;
1262
1263		if (allow_pagemode && scan->rs_base.rs_snapshot &&
1264			IsMVCCSnapshot(scan->rs_base.rs_snapshot))
1265			scan->rs_base.rs_flags |= SO_ALLOW_PAGEMODE;
1266		else
1267			scan->rs_base.rs_flags &= ~SO_ALLOW_PAGEMODE;
1268	}
1269
1270	/*
1271	 * unpin scan buffers
1272	 */
1273	if (BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))
1274	{
1275		ReleaseBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf);
1276		scan->rs_cbuf = InvalidBuffer;
1277	}
1278
1279	/*
1280	 * SO_TYPE_BITMAPSCAN would be cleaned up here, but it does not hold any
1281	 * additional data vs a normal HeapScan
1282	 */
1283
1284	/*
1285	 * The read stream is reset on rescan. This must be done before
1286	 * initscan(), as some state referred to by read_stream_reset() is reset
1287	 * in initscan().
1288	 */
1289	if (scan->rs_read_stream)
1290		read_stream_reset(scan->rs_read_stream);
1291
1292	/*
1293	 * reinitialize scan descriptor
1294	 */
1295	initscan(scan, key, true);
1296}
1297
1298void
1299heap_endscan(TableScanDesc sscan)
1300{
1301	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) sscan;
1302
1303	/* Note: no locking manipulations needed */
1304
1305	/*
1306	 * unpin scan buffers
1307	 */
1308	if (BufferIsValid(scan->rs_cbuf))
1309		ReleaseBuffer(scan->rs_cbuf);
1310
1311	/*
1312	 * Must free the read stream before freeing the BufferAccessStrategy.
1313	 */
1314	if (scan->rs_read_stream)
1315		read_stream_end(scan->rs_read_stream);
1316
1317	/*
1318	 * decrement relation reference count and free scan descriptor storage
1319	 */
1320	RelationDecrementReferenceCount(scan->rs_base.rs_rd);
1321
1322	if (scan->rs_base.rs_key)
1323		pfree(scan->rs_base.rs_key);
1324
1325	if (scan->rs_strategy != NULL)
1326		FreeAccessStrategy(scan->rs_strategy);
1327
1328	if (scan->rs_parallelworkerdata != NULL)
1329		pfree(scan->rs_parallelworkerdata);
1330
1331	if (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_TEMP_SNAPSHOT)
1332		UnregisterSnapshot(scan->rs_base.rs_snapshot);
1333
1334	pfree(scan);
1335}
1336
1337HeapTuple
1338heap_getnext(TableScanDesc sscan, ScanDirection direction)
1339{
1340	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) sscan;
1341
1342	/*
1343	 * This is still widely used directly, without going through table AM, so
1344	 * add a safety check.  It's possible we should, at a later point,
1345	 * downgrade this to an assert. The reason for checking the AM routine,
1346	 * rather than the AM oid, is that this allows to write regression tests
1347	 * that create another AM reusing the heap handler.
1348	 */
1349	if (unlikely(sscan->rs_rd->rd_tableam != GetHeapamTableAmRoutine()))
1350		ereport(ERROR,
1351				(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
1352				 errmsg_internal("only heap AM is supported")));
1353
1354	/*
1355	 * We don't expect direct calls to heap_getnext with valid CheckXidAlive
1356	 * for catalog or regular tables.  See detailed comments in xact.c where
1357	 * these variables are declared.  Normally we have such a check at tableam
1358	 * level API but this is called from many places so we need to ensure it
1359	 * here.
1360	 */
1361	if (unlikely(TransactionIdIsValid(CheckXidAlive) && !bsysscan))
1362		elog(ERROR, "unexpected heap_getnext call during logical decoding");
1363
1364	/* Note: no locking manipulations needed */
1365
1366	if (scan->rs_base.rs_flags & SO_ALLOW_PAGEMODE)
1367		heapgettup_pagemode(scan, direction,
1368							scan->rs_base.rs_nkeys, scan->rs_base.rs_key);
1369	else
1370		heapgettup(scan, direction,
1371				   scan->rs_base.rs_nkeys, scan->rs_base.rs_key);
1372
1373	if (scan->rs_ctup.t_data == NULL)
1374		return NULL;
1375
1376	/*
1377	 * if we get here it means we have a new current scan tuple, so point to
1378	 * the proper return buffer and return the tuple.
1379	 */
1380
1381	pgstat_count_heap_getnext(scan->rs_base.rs_rd);
1382
1383	return &scan->rs_ctup;
1384}
1385
1386bool
1387heap_getnextslot(TableScanDesc sscan, ScanDirection direction, TupleTableSlot *slot)
1388{
1389	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) sscan;
1390
1391	/* Note: no locking manipulations needed */
1392
1393	if (sscan->rs_flags & SO_ALLOW_PAGEMODE)
1394		heapgettup_pagemode(scan, direction, sscan->rs_nkeys, sscan->rs_key);
1395	else
1396		heapgettup(scan, direction, sscan->rs_nkeys, sscan->rs_key);
1397
1398	if (scan->rs_ctup.t_data == NULL)
1399	{
1400		ExecClearTuple(slot);
1401		return false;
1402	}
1403
1404	/*
1405	 * if we get here it means we have a new current scan tuple, so point to
1406	 * the proper return buffer and return the tuple.
1407	 */
1408
1409	pgstat_count_heap_getnext(scan->rs_base.rs_rd);
1410
1411	ExecStoreBufferHeapTuple(&scan->rs_ctup, slot,
1412							 scan->rs_cbuf);
1413	return true;
1414}
1415
1416void
1417heap_set_tidrange(TableScanDesc sscan, ItemPointer mintid,
1418				  ItemPointer maxtid)
1419{
1420	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) sscan;
1421	BlockNumber startBlk;
1422	BlockNumber numBlks;
1423	ItemPointerData highestItem;
1424	ItemPointerData lowestItem;
1425
1426	/*
1427	 * For relations without any pages, we can simply leave the TID range
1428	 * unset.  There will be no tuples to scan, therefore no tuples outside
1429	 * the given TID range.
1430	 */
1431	if (scan->rs_nblocks == 0)
1432		return;
1433
1434	/*
1435	 * Set up some ItemPointers which point to the first and last possible
1436	 * tuples in the heap.
1437	 */
1438	ItemPointerSet(&highestItem, scan->rs_nblocks - 1, MaxOffsetNumber);
1439	ItemPointerSet(&lowestItem, 0, FirstOffsetNumber);
1440
1441	/*
1442	 * If the given maximum TID is below the highest possible TID in the
1443	 * relation, then restrict the range to that, otherwise we scan to the end
1444	 * of the relation.
1445	 */
1446	if (ItemPointerCompare(maxtid, &highestItem) < 0)
1447		ItemPointerCopy(maxtid, &highestItem);
1448
1449	/*
1450	 * If the given minimum TID is above the lowest possible TID in the
1451	 * relation, then restrict the range to only scan for TIDs above that.
1452	 */
1453	if (ItemPointerCompare(mintid, &lowestItem) > 0)
1454		ItemPointerCopy(mintid, &lowestItem);
1455
1456	/*
1457	 * Check for an empty range and protect from would be negative results
1458	 * from the numBlks calculation below.
1459	 */
1460	if (ItemPointerCompare(&highestItem, &lowestItem) < 0)
1461	{
1462		/* Set an empty range of blocks to scan */
1463		heap_setscanlimits(sscan, 0, 0);
1464		return;
1465	}
1466
1467	/*
1468	 * Calculate the first block and the number of blocks we must scan. We
1469	 * could be more aggressive here and perform some more validation to try
1470	 * and further narrow the scope of blocks to scan by checking if the
1471	 * lowestItem has an offset above MaxOffsetNumber.  In this case, we could
1472	 * advance startBlk by one.  Likewise, if highestItem has an offset of 0
1473	 * we could scan one fewer blocks.  However, such an optimization does not
1474	 * seem worth troubling over, currently.
1475	 */
1476	startBlk = ItemPointerGetBlockNumberNoCheck(&lowestItem);
1477
1478	numBlks = ItemPointerGetBlockNumberNoCheck(&highestItem) -
1479		ItemPointerGetBlockNumberNoCheck(&lowestItem) + 1;
1480
1481	/* Set the start block and number of blocks to scan */
1482	heap_setscanlimits(sscan, startBlk, numBlks);
1483
1484	/* Finally, set the TID range in sscan */
1485	ItemPointerCopy(&lowestItem, &sscan->st.tidrange.rs_mintid);
1486	ItemPointerCopy(&highestItem, &sscan->st.tidrange.rs_maxtid);
1487}
1488
1489bool
1490heap_getnextslot_tidrange(TableScanDesc sscan, ScanDirection direction,
1491						  TupleTableSlot *slot)
1492{
1493	HeapScanDesc scan = (HeapScanDesc) sscan;
1494	ItemPointer mintid = &sscan->st.tidrange.rs_mintid;
1495	ItemPointer maxtid = &sscan->st.tidrange.rs_maxtid;
1496
1497	/* Note: no locking manipulations needed */
1498	for (;;)
1499	{
1500		if (sscan->rs_flags & SO_ALLOW_PAGEMODE)
1501			heapgettup_pagemode(scan, direction, sscan->rs_nkeys, sscan->rs_key);
1502		else
1503			heapgettup(scan, direction, sscan->rs_nkeys, sscan->rs_key);
1504
1505		if (scan->rs_ctup.t_data == NULL)
1506		{
1507			ExecClearTuple(slot);
1508			return false;
1509		}
1510
1511		/*
1512		 * heap_set_tidrange will have used heap_setscanlimits to limit the
1513		 * range of pages we scan to only ones that can contain the TID range
1514		 * we're scanning for.  Here we must filter out any tuples from these
1515		 * pages that are outside of that range.
1516		 */
1517		if (ItemPointerCompare(&scan->rs_ctup.t_self, mintid) < 0)
1518		{
1519			ExecClearTuple(slot);
1520
1521			/*
1522			 * When scanning backwards, the TIDs will be in descending order.
1523			 * Future tuples in this direction will be lower still, so we can
1524			 * just return false to indicate there will be no more tuples.
1525			 */
1526			if (ScanDirectionIsBackward(direction))
1527				return false;
1528
1529			continue;
1530		}
1531
1532		/*
1533		 * Likewise for the final page, we must filter out TIDs greater than
1534		 * maxtid.
1535		 */
1536		if (ItemPointerCompare(&scan->rs_ctup.t_self, maxtid) > 0)
1537		{
1538			ExecClearTuple(slot);
1539
1540			/*
1541			 * When scanning forward, the TIDs will be in ascending order.
1542			 * Future tuples in this direction will be higher still, so we can
1543			 * just return false to indicate there will be no more tuples.
1544			 */
1545			if (ScanDirectionIsForward(direction))
1546				return false;
1547			continue;
1548		}
1549
1550		break;
1551	}
1552
1553	/*
1554	 * if we get here it means we have a new current scan tuple, so point to
1555	 * the proper return buffer and return the tuple.
1556	 */
1557	pgstat_count_heap_getnext(scan->rs_base.rs_rd);
1558
1559	ExecStoreBufferHeapTuple(&scan->rs_ctup, slot, scan->rs_cbuf);
1560	return true;
1561}
1562
1563/*
1564 *	heap_fetch		- retrieve tuple with given tid
1565 *
1566 * On entry, tuple->t_self is the TID to fetch.  We pin the buffer holding
1567 * the tuple, fill in the remaining fields of *tuple, and check the tuple
1568 * against the specified snapshot.
1569 *
1570 * If successful (tuple found and passes snapshot time qual), then *userbuf
1571 * is set to the buffer holding the tuple and true is returned.  The caller
1572 * must unpin the buffer when done with the tuple.
1573 *
1574 * If the tuple is not found (ie, item number references a deleted slot),
1575 * then tuple->t_data is set to NULL, *userbuf is set to InvalidBuffer,
1576 * and false is returned.
1577 *
1578 * If the tuple is found but fails the time qual check, then the behavior
1579 * depends on the keep_buf parameter.  If keep_buf is false, the results
1580 * are the same as for the tuple-not-found case.  If keep_buf is true,
1581 * then tuple->t_data and *userbuf are returned as for the success case,
1582 * and again the caller must unpin the buffer; but false is returned.
1583 *
1584 * heap_fetch does not follow HOT chains: only the exact TID requested will
1585 * be fetched.
1586 *
1587 * It is somewhat inconsistent that we ereport() on invalid block number but
1588 * return false on invalid item number.  There are a couple of reasons though.
1589 * One is that the caller can relatively easily check the block number for
1590 * validity, but cannot check the item number without reading the page
1591 * himself.  Another is that when we are following a t_ctid link, we can be
1592 * reasonably confident that the page number is valid (since VACUUM shouldn't
1593 * truncate off the destination page without having killed the referencing
1594 * tuple first), but the item number might well not be good.
1595 */
1596bool
1597heap_fetch(Relation relation,
1598		   Snapshot snapshot,
1599		   HeapTuple tuple,
1600		   Buffer *userbuf,
1601		   bool keep_buf)
1602{
1603	ItemPointer tid = &(tuple->t_self);
1604	ItemId		lp;
1605	Buffer		buffer;
1606	Page		page;
1607	OffsetNumber offnum;
1608	bool		valid;
1609
1610	/*
1611	 * Fetch and pin the appropriate page of the relation.
1612	 */
1613	buffer = ReadBuffer(relation, ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid));
1614
1615	/*
1616	 * Need share lock on buffer to examine tuple commit status.
1617	 */
1618	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE);
1619	page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
1620
1621	/*
1622	 * We'd better check for out-of-range offnum in case of VACUUM since the
1623	 * TID was obtained.
1624	 */
1625	offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(tid);
1626	if (offnum < FirstOffsetNumber || offnum > PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page))
1627	{
1628		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
1629		ReleaseBuffer(buffer);
1630		*userbuf = InvalidBuffer;
1631		tuple->t_data = NULL;
1632		return false;
1633	}
1634
1635	/*
1636	 * get the item line pointer corresponding to the requested tid
1637	 */
1638	lp = PageGetItemId(page, offnum);
1639
1640	/*
1641	 * Must check for deleted tuple.
1642	 */
1643	if (!ItemIdIsNormal(lp))
1644	{
1645		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
1646		ReleaseBuffer(buffer);
1647		*userbuf = InvalidBuffer;
1648		tuple->t_data = NULL;
1649		return false;
1650	}
1651
1652	/*
1653	 * fill in *tuple fields
1654	 */
1655	tuple->t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lp);
1656	tuple->t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lp);
1657	tuple->t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
1658
1659	/*
1660	 * check tuple visibility, then release lock
1661	 */
1662	valid = HeapTupleSatisfiesVisibility(tuple, snapshot, buffer);
1663
1664	if (valid)
1665		PredicateLockTID(relation, &(tuple->t_self), snapshot,
1666						 HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(tuple->t_data));
1667
1668	HeapCheckForSerializableConflictOut(valid, relation, tuple, buffer, snapshot);
1669
1670	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
1671
1672	if (valid)
1673	{
1674		/*
1675		 * All checks passed, so return the tuple as valid. Caller is now
1676		 * responsible for releasing the buffer.
1677		 */
1678		*userbuf = buffer;
1679
1680		return true;
1681	}
1682
1683	/* Tuple failed time qual, but maybe caller wants to see it anyway. */
1684	if (keep_buf)
1685		*userbuf = buffer;
1686	else
1687	{
1688		ReleaseBuffer(buffer);
1689		*userbuf = InvalidBuffer;
1690		tuple->t_data = NULL;
1691	}
1692
1693	return false;
1694}
1695
1696/*
1697 *	heap_hot_search_buffer	- search HOT chain for tuple satisfying snapshot
1698 *
1699 * On entry, *tid is the TID of a tuple (either a simple tuple, or the root
1700 * of a HOT chain), and buffer is the buffer holding this tuple.  We search
1701 * for the first chain member satisfying the given snapshot.  If one is
1702 * found, we update *tid to reference that tuple's offset number, and
1703 * return true.  If no match, return false without modifying *tid.
1704 *
1705 * heapTuple is a caller-supplied buffer.  When a match is found, we return
1706 * the tuple here, in addition to updating *tid.  If no match is found, the
1707 * contents of this buffer on return are undefined.
1708 *
1709 * If all_dead is not NULL, we check non-visible tuples to see if they are
1710 * globally dead; *all_dead is set true if all members of the HOT chain
1711 * are vacuumable, false if not.
1712 *
1713 * Unlike heap_fetch, the caller must already have pin and (at least) share
1714 * lock on the buffer; it is still pinned/locked at exit.
1715 */
1716bool
1717heap_hot_search_buffer(ItemPointer tid, Relation relation, Buffer buffer,
1718					   Snapshot snapshot, HeapTuple heapTuple,
1719					   bool *all_dead, bool first_call)
1720{
1721	Page		page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
1722	TransactionId prev_xmax = InvalidTransactionId;
1723	BlockNumber blkno;
1724	OffsetNumber offnum;
1725	bool		at_chain_start;
1726	bool		valid;
1727	bool		skip;
1728	GlobalVisState *vistest = NULL;
1729
1730	/* If this is not the first call, previous call returned a (live!) tuple */
1731	if (all_dead)
1732		*all_dead = first_call;
1733
1734	blkno = ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid);
1735	offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(tid);
1736	at_chain_start = first_call;
1737	skip = !first_call;
1738
1739	/* XXX: we should assert that a snapshot is pushed or registered */
1740	Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(RecentXmin));
1741	Assert(BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer) == blkno);
1742
1743	/* Scan through possible multiple members of HOT-chain */
1744	for (;;)
1745	{
1746		ItemId		lp;
1747
1748		/* check for bogus TID */
1749		if (offnum < FirstOffsetNumber || offnum > PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page))
1750			break;
1751
1752		lp = PageGetItemId(page, offnum);
1753
1754		/* check for unused, dead, or redirected items */
1755		if (!ItemIdIsNormal(lp))
1756		{
1757			/* We should only see a redirect at start of chain */
1758			if (ItemIdIsRedirected(lp) && at_chain_start)
1759			{
1760				/* Follow the redirect */
1761				offnum = ItemIdGetRedirect(lp);
1762				at_chain_start = false;
1763				continue;
1764			}
1765			/* else must be end of chain */
1766			break;
1767		}
1768
1769		/*
1770		 * Update heapTuple to point to the element of the HOT chain we're
1771		 * currently investigating. Having t_self set correctly is important
1772		 * because the SSI checks and the *Satisfies routine for historical
1773		 * MVCC snapshots need the correct tid to decide about the visibility.
1774		 */
1775		heapTuple->t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lp);
1776		heapTuple->t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lp);
1777		heapTuple->t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
1778		ItemPointerSet(&heapTuple->t_self, blkno, offnum);
1779
1780		/*
1781		 * Shouldn't see a HEAP_ONLY tuple at chain start.
1782		 */
1783		if (at_chain_start && HeapTupleIsHeapOnly(heapTuple))
1784			break;
1785
1786		/*
1787		 * The xmin should match the previous xmax value, else chain is
1788		 * broken.
1789		 */
1790		if (TransactionIdIsValid(prev_xmax) &&
1791			!TransactionIdEquals(prev_xmax,
1792								 HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(heapTuple->t_data)))
1793			break;
1794
1795		/*
1796		 * When first_call is true (and thus, skip is initially false) we'll
1797		 * return the first tuple we find.  But on later passes, heapTuple
1798		 * will initially be pointing to the tuple we returned last time.
1799		 * Returning it again would be incorrect (and would loop forever), so
1800		 * we skip it and return the next match we find.
1801		 */
1802		if (!skip)
1803		{
1804			/* If it's visible per the snapshot, we must return it */
1805			valid = HeapTupleSatisfiesVisibility(heapTuple, snapshot, buffer);
1806			HeapCheckForSerializableConflictOut(valid, relation, heapTuple,
1807												buffer, snapshot);
1808
1809			if (valid)
1810			{
1811				ItemPointerSetOffsetNumber(tid, offnum);
1812				PredicateLockTID(relation, &heapTuple->t_self, snapshot,
1813								 HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(heapTuple->t_data));
1814				if (all_dead)
1815					*all_dead = false;
1816				return true;
1817			}
1818		}
1819		skip = false;
1820
1821		/*
1822		 * If we can't see it, maybe no one else can either.  At caller
1823		 * request, check whether all chain members are dead to all
1824		 * transactions.
1825		 *
1826		 * Note: if you change the criterion here for what is "dead", fix the
1827		 * planner's get_actual_variable_range() function to match.
1828		 */
1829		if (all_dead && *all_dead)
1830		{
1831			if (!vistest)
1832				vistest = GlobalVisTestFor(relation);
1833
1834			if (!HeapTupleIsSurelyDead(heapTuple, vistest))
1835				*all_dead = false;
1836		}
1837
1838		/*
1839		 * Check to see if HOT chain continues past this tuple; if so fetch
1840		 * the next offnum and loop around.
1841		 */
1842		if (HeapTupleIsHotUpdated(heapTuple))
1843		{
1844			Assert(ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&heapTuple->t_data->t_ctid) ==
1845				   blkno);
1846			offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&heapTuple->t_data->t_ctid);
1847			at_chain_start = false;
1848			prev_xmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetUpdateXid(heapTuple->t_data);
1849		}
1850		else
1851			break;				/* end of chain */
1852	}
1853
1854	return false;
1855}
1856
1857/*
1858 *	heap_get_latest_tid -  get the latest tid of a specified tuple
1859 *
1860 * Actually, this gets the latest version that is visible according to the
1861 * scan's snapshot.  Create a scan using SnapshotDirty to get the very latest,
1862 * possibly uncommitted version.
1863 *
1864 * *tid is both an input and an output parameter: it is updated to
1865 * show the latest version of the row.  Note that it will not be changed
1866 * if no version of the row passes the snapshot test.
1867 */
1868void
1869heap_get_latest_tid(TableScanDesc sscan,
1870					ItemPointer tid)
1871{
1872	Relation	relation = sscan->rs_rd;
1873	Snapshot	snapshot = sscan->rs_snapshot;
1874	ItemPointerData ctid;
1875	TransactionId priorXmax;
1876
1877	/*
1878	 * table_tuple_get_latest_tid() verified that the passed in tid is valid.
1879	 * Assume that t_ctid links are valid however - there shouldn't be invalid
1880	 * ones in the table.
1881	 */
1882	Assert(ItemPointerIsValid(tid));
1883
1884	/*
1885	 * Loop to chase down t_ctid links.  At top of loop, ctid is the tuple we
1886	 * need to examine, and *tid is the TID we will return if ctid turns out
1887	 * to be bogus.
1888	 *
1889	 * Note that we will loop until we reach the end of the t_ctid chain.
1890	 * Depending on the snapshot passed, there might be at most one visible
1891	 * version of the row, but we don't try to optimize for that.
1892	 */
1893	ctid = *tid;
1894	priorXmax = InvalidTransactionId;	/* cannot check first XMIN */
1895	for (;;)
1896	{
1897		Buffer		buffer;
1898		Page		page;
1899		OffsetNumber offnum;
1900		ItemId		lp;
1901		HeapTupleData tp;
1902		bool		valid;
1903
1904		/*
1905		 * Read, pin, and lock the page.
1906		 */
1907		buffer = ReadBuffer(relation, ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&ctid));
1908		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_SHARE);
1909		page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
1910
1911		/*
1912		 * Check for bogus item number.  This is not treated as an error
1913		 * condition because it can happen while following a t_ctid link. We
1914		 * just assume that the prior tid is OK and return it unchanged.
1915		 */
1916		offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&ctid);
1917		if (offnum < FirstOffsetNumber || offnum > PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page))
1918		{
1919			UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
1920			break;
1921		}
1922		lp = PageGetItemId(page, offnum);
1923		if (!ItemIdIsNormal(lp))
1924		{
1925			UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
1926			break;
1927		}
1928
1929		/* OK to access the tuple */
1930		tp.t_self = ctid;
1931		tp.t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lp);
1932		tp.t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lp);
1933		tp.t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
1934
1935		/*
1936		 * After following a t_ctid link, we might arrive at an unrelated
1937		 * tuple.  Check for XMIN match.
1938		 */
1939		if (TransactionIdIsValid(priorXmax) &&
1940			!TransactionIdEquals(priorXmax, HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(tp.t_data)))
1941		{
1942			UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
1943			break;
1944		}
1945
1946		/*
1947		 * Check tuple visibility; if visible, set it as the new result
1948		 * candidate.
1949		 */
1950		valid = HeapTupleSatisfiesVisibility(&tp, snapshot, buffer);
1951		HeapCheckForSerializableConflictOut(valid, relation, &tp, buffer, snapshot);
1952		if (valid)
1953			*tid = ctid;
1954
1955		/*
1956		 * If there's a valid t_ctid link, follow it, else we're done.
1957		 */
1958		if ((tp.t_data->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_INVALID) ||
1959			HeapTupleHeaderIsOnlyLocked(tp.t_data) ||
1960			HeapTupleHeaderIndicatesMovedPartitions(tp.t_data) ||
1961			ItemPointerEquals(&tp.t_self, &tp.t_data->t_ctid))
1962		{
1963			UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
1964			break;
1965		}
1966
1967		ctid = tp.t_data->t_ctid;
1968		priorXmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetUpdateXid(tp.t_data);
1969		UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
1970	}							/* end of loop */
1971}
1972
1973
1974/*
1975 * UpdateXmaxHintBits - update tuple hint bits after xmax transaction ends
1976 *
1977 * This is called after we have waited for the XMAX transaction to terminate.
1978 * If the transaction aborted, we guarantee the XMAX_INVALID hint bit will
1979 * be set on exit.  If the transaction committed, we set the XMAX_COMMITTED
1980 * hint bit if possible --- but beware that that may not yet be possible,
1981 * if the transaction committed asynchronously.
1982 *
1983 * Note that if the transaction was a locker only, we set HEAP_XMAX_INVALID
1984 * even if it commits.
1985 *
1986 * Hence callers should look only at XMAX_INVALID.
1987 *
1988 * Note this is not allowed for tuples whose xmax is a multixact.
1989 */
1990static void
1991UpdateXmaxHintBits(HeapTupleHeader tuple, Buffer buffer, TransactionId xid)
1992{
1993	Assert(TransactionIdEquals(HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(tuple), xid));
1994	Assert(!(tuple->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_IS_MULTI));
1995
1996	if (!(tuple->t_infomask & (HEAP_XMAX_COMMITTED | HEAP_XMAX_INVALID)))
1997	{
1998		if (!HEAP_XMAX_IS_LOCKED_ONLY(tuple->t_infomask) &&
1999			TransactionIdDidCommit(xid))
2000			HeapTupleSetHintBits(tuple, buffer, HEAP_XMAX_COMMITTED,
2001								 xid);
2002		else
2003			HeapTupleSetHintBits(tuple, buffer, HEAP_XMAX_INVALID,
2004								 InvalidTransactionId);
2005	}
2006}
2007
2008
2009/*
2010 * GetBulkInsertState - prepare status object for a bulk insert
2011 */
2012BulkInsertState
2013GetBulkInsertState(void)
2014{
2015	BulkInsertState bistate;
2016
2017	bistate = (BulkInsertState) palloc(sizeof(BulkInsertStateData));
2018	bistate->strategy = GetAccessStrategy(BAS_BULKWRITE);
2019	bistate->current_buf = InvalidBuffer;
2020	bistate->next_free = InvalidBlockNumber;
2021	bistate->last_free = InvalidBlockNumber;
2022	bistate->already_extended_by = 0;
2023	return bistate;
2024}
2025
2026/*
2027 * FreeBulkInsertState - clean up after finishing a bulk insert
2028 */
2029void
2030FreeBulkInsertState(BulkInsertState bistate)
2031{
2032	if (bistate->current_buf != InvalidBuffer)
2033		ReleaseBuffer(bistate->current_buf);
2034	FreeAccessStrategy(bistate->strategy);
2035	pfree(bistate);
2036}
2037
2038/*
2039 * ReleaseBulkInsertStatePin - release a buffer currently held in bistate
2040 */
2041void
2042ReleaseBulkInsertStatePin(BulkInsertState bistate)
2043{
2044	if (bistate->current_buf != InvalidBuffer)
2045		ReleaseBuffer(bistate->current_buf);
2046	bistate->current_buf = InvalidBuffer;
2047
2048	/*
2049	 * Despite the name, we also reset bulk relation extension state.
2050	 * Otherwise we can end up erroring out due to looking for free space in
2051	 * ->next_free of one partition, even though ->next_free was set when
2052	 * extending another partition. It could obviously also be bad for
2053	 * efficiency to look at existing blocks at offsets from another
2054	 * partition, even if we don't error out.
2055	 */
2056	bistate->next_free = InvalidBlockNumber;
2057	bistate->last_free = InvalidBlockNumber;
2058}
2059
2060
2061/*
2062 *	heap_insert		- insert tuple into a heap
2063 *
2064 * The new tuple is stamped with current transaction ID and the specified
2065 * command ID.
2066 *
2067 * See table_tuple_insert for comments about most of the input flags, except
2068 * that this routine directly takes a tuple rather than a slot.
2069 *
2070 * There's corresponding HEAP_INSERT_ options to all the TABLE_INSERT_
2071 * options, and there additionally is HEAP_INSERT_SPECULATIVE which is used to
2072 * implement table_tuple_insert_speculative().
2073 *
2074 * On return the header fields of *tup are updated to match the stored tuple;
2075 * in particular tup->t_self receives the actual TID where the tuple was
2076 * stored.  But note that any toasting of fields within the tuple data is NOT
2077 * reflected into *tup.
2078 */
2079void
2080heap_insert(Relation relation, HeapTuple tup, CommandId cid,
2081			int options, BulkInsertState bistate)
2082{
2083	TransactionId xid = GetCurrentTransactionId();
2084	HeapTuple	heaptup;
2085	Buffer		buffer;
2086	Buffer		vmbuffer = InvalidBuffer;
2087	bool		all_visible_cleared = false;
2088
2089	/* Cheap, simplistic check that the tuple matches the rel's rowtype. */
2090	Assert(HeapTupleHeaderGetNatts(tup->t_data) <=
2091		   RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(relation));
2092
2093	AssertHasSnapshotForToast(relation);
2094
2095	/*
2096	 * Fill in tuple header fields and toast the tuple if necessary.
2097	 *
2098	 * Note: below this point, heaptup is the data we actually intend to store
2099	 * into the relation; tup is the caller's original untoasted data.
2100	 */
2101	heaptup = heap_prepare_insert(relation, tup, xid, cid, options);
2102
2103	/*
2104	 * Find buffer to insert this tuple into.  If the page is all visible,
2105	 * this will also pin the requisite visibility map page.
2106	 */
2107	buffer = RelationGetBufferForTuple(relation, heaptup->t_len,
2108									   InvalidBuffer, options, bistate,
2109									   &vmbuffer, NULL,
2110									   0);
2111
2112	/*
2113	 * We're about to do the actual insert -- but check for conflict first, to
2114	 * avoid possibly having to roll back work we've just done.
2115	 *
2116	 * This is safe without a recheck as long as there is no possibility of
2117	 * another process scanning the page between this check and the insert
2118	 * being visible to the scan (i.e., an exclusive buffer content lock is
2119	 * continuously held from this point until the tuple insert is visible).
2120	 *
2121	 * For a heap insert, we only need to check for table-level SSI locks. Our
2122	 * new tuple can't possibly conflict with existing tuple locks, and heap
2123	 * page locks are only consolidated versions of tuple locks; they do not
2124	 * lock "gaps" as index page locks do.  So we don't need to specify a
2125	 * buffer when making the call, which makes for a faster check.
2126	 */
2127	CheckForSerializableConflictIn(relation, NULL, InvalidBlockNumber);
2128
2129	/* NO EREPORT(ERROR) from here till changes are logged */
2130	START_CRIT_SECTION();
2131
2132	RelationPutHeapTuple(relation, buffer, heaptup,
2133						 (options & HEAP_INSERT_SPECULATIVE) != 0);
2134
2135	if (PageIsAllVisible(BufferGetPage(buffer)))
2136	{
2137		all_visible_cleared = true;
2138		PageClearAllVisible(BufferGetPage(buffer));
2139		visibilitymap_clear(relation,
2140							ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&(heaptup->t_self)),
2141							vmbuffer, VISIBILITYMAP_VALID_BITS);
2142	}
2143
2144	/*
2145	 * XXX Should we set PageSetPrunable on this page ?
2146	 *
2147	 * The inserting transaction may eventually abort thus making this tuple
2148	 * DEAD and hence available for pruning. Though we don't want to optimize
2149	 * for aborts, if no other tuple in this page is UPDATEd/DELETEd, the
2150	 * aborted tuple will never be pruned until next vacuum is triggered.
2151	 *
2152	 * If you do add PageSetPrunable here, add it in heap_xlog_insert too.
2153	 */
2154
2155	MarkBufferDirty(buffer);
2156
2157	/* XLOG stuff */
2158	if (RelationNeedsWAL(relation))
2159	{
2160		xl_heap_insert xlrec;
2161		xl_heap_header xlhdr;
2162		XLogRecPtr	recptr;
2163		Page		page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
2164		uint8		info = XLOG_HEAP_INSERT;
2165		int			bufflags = 0;
2166
2167		/*
2168		 * If this is a catalog, we need to transmit combo CIDs to properly
2169		 * decode, so log that as well.
2170		 */
2171		if (RelationIsAccessibleInLogicalDecoding(relation))
2172			log_heap_new_cid(relation, heaptup);
2173
2174		/*
2175		 * If this is the single and first tuple on page, we can reinit the
2176		 * page instead of restoring the whole thing.  Set flag, and hide
2177		 * buffer references from XLogInsert.
2178		 */
2179		if (ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&(heaptup->t_self)) == FirstOffsetNumber &&
2180			PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) == FirstOffsetNumber)
2181		{
2182			info |= XLOG_HEAP_INIT_PAGE;
2183			bufflags |= REGBUF_WILL_INIT;
2184		}
2185
2186		xlrec.offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&heaptup->t_self);
2187		xlrec.flags = 0;
2188		if (all_visible_cleared)
2189			xlrec.flags |= XLH_INSERT_ALL_VISIBLE_CLEARED;
2190		if (options & HEAP_INSERT_SPECULATIVE)
2191			xlrec.flags |= XLH_INSERT_IS_SPECULATIVE;
2192		Assert(ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&heaptup->t_self) == BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer));
2193
2194		/*
2195		 * For logical decoding, we need the tuple even if we're doing a full
2196		 * page write, so make sure it's included even if we take a full-page
2197		 * image. (XXX We could alternatively store a pointer into the FPW).
2198		 */
2199		if (RelationIsLogicallyLogged(relation) &&
2200			!(options & HEAP_INSERT_NO_LOGICAL))
2201		{
2202			xlrec.flags |= XLH_INSERT_CONTAINS_NEW_TUPLE;
2203			bufflags |= REGBUF_KEEP_DATA;
2204
2205			if (IsToastRelation(relation))
2206				xlrec.flags |= XLH_INSERT_ON_TOAST_RELATION;
2207		}
2208
2209		XLogBeginInsert();
2210		XLogRegisterData(&xlrec, SizeOfHeapInsert);
2211
2212		xlhdr.t_infomask2 = heaptup->t_data->t_infomask2;
2213		xlhdr.t_infomask = heaptup->t_data->t_infomask;
2214		xlhdr.t_hoff = heaptup->t_data->t_hoff;
2215
2216		/*
2217		 * note we mark xlhdr as belonging to buffer; if XLogInsert decides to
2218		 * write the whole page to the xlog, we don't need to store
2219		 * xl_heap_header in the xlog.
2220		 */
2221		XLogRegisterBuffer(0, buffer, REGBUF_STANDARD | bufflags);
2222		XLogRegisterBufData(0, &xlhdr, SizeOfHeapHeader);
2223		/* PG73FORMAT: write bitmap [+ padding] [+ oid] + data */
2224		XLogRegisterBufData(0,
2225							(char *) heaptup->t_data + SizeofHeapTupleHeader,
2226							heaptup->t_len - SizeofHeapTupleHeader);
2227
2228		/* filtering by origin on a row level is much more efficient */
2229		XLogSetRecordFlags(XLOG_INCLUDE_ORIGIN);
2230
2231		recptr = XLogInsert(RM_HEAP_ID, info);
2232
2233		PageSetLSN(page, recptr);
2234	}
2235
2236	END_CRIT_SECTION();
2237
2238	UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
2239	if (vmbuffer != InvalidBuffer)
2240		ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
2241
2242	/*
2243	 * If tuple is cachable, mark it for invalidation from the caches in case
2244	 * we abort.  Note it is OK to do this after releasing the buffer, because
2245	 * the heaptup data structure is all in local memory, not in the shared
2246	 * buffer.
2247	 */
2248	CacheInvalidateHeapTuple(relation, heaptup, NULL);
2249
2250	/* Note: speculative insertions are counted too, even if aborted later */
2251	pgstat_count_heap_insert(relation, 1);
2252
2253	/*
2254	 * If heaptup is a private copy, release it.  Don't forget to copy t_self
2255	 * back to the caller's image, too.
2256	 */
2257	if (heaptup != tup)
2258	{
2259		tup->t_self = heaptup->t_self;
2260		heap_freetuple(heaptup);
2261	}
2262}
2263
2264/*
2265 * Subroutine for heap_insert(). Prepares a tuple for insertion. This sets the
2266 * tuple header fields and toasts the tuple if necessary.  Returns a toasted
2267 * version of the tuple if it was toasted, or the original tuple if not. Note
2268 * that in any case, the header fields are also set in the original tuple.
2269 */
2270static HeapTuple
2271heap_prepare_insert(Relation relation, HeapTuple tup, TransactionId xid,
2272					CommandId cid, int options)
2273{
2274	/*
2275	 * To allow parallel inserts, we need to ensure that they are safe to be
2276	 * performed in workers. We have the infrastructure to allow parallel
2277	 * inserts in general except for the cases where inserts generate a new
2278	 * CommandId (eg. inserts into a table having a foreign key column).
2279	 */
2280	if (IsParallelWorker())
2281		ereport(ERROR,
2282				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TRANSACTION_STATE),
2283				 errmsg("cannot insert tuples in a parallel worker")));
2284
2285	tup->t_data->t_infomask &= ~(HEAP_XACT_MASK);
2286	tup->t_data->t_infomask2 &= ~(HEAP2_XACT_MASK);
2287	tup->t_data->t_infomask |= HEAP_XMAX_INVALID;
2288	HeapTupleHeaderSetXmin(tup->t_data, xid);
2289	if (options & HEAP_INSERT_FROZEN)
2290		HeapTupleHeaderSetXminFrozen(tup->t_data);
2291
2292	HeapTupleHeaderSetCmin(tup->t_data, cid);
2293	HeapTupleHeaderSetXmax(tup->t_data, 0); /* for cleanliness */
2294	tup->t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
2295
2296	/*
2297	 * If the new tuple is too big for storage or contains already toasted
2298	 * out-of-line attributes from some other relation, invoke the toaster.
2299	 */
2300	if (relation->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
2301		relation->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_MATVIEW)
2302	{
2303		/* toast table entries should never be recursively toasted */
2304		Assert(!HeapTupleHasExternal(tup));
2305		return tup;
2306	}
2307	else if (HeapTupleHasExternal(tup) || tup->t_len > TOAST_TUPLE_THRESHOLD)
2308		return heap_toast_insert_or_update(relation, tup, NULL, options);
2309	else
2310		return tup;
2311}
2312
2313/*
2314 * Helper for heap_multi_insert() that computes the number of entire pages
2315 * that inserting the remaining heaptuples requires. Used to determine how
2316 * much the relation needs to be extended by.
2317 */
2318static int
2319heap_multi_insert_pages(HeapTuple *heaptuples, int done, int ntuples, Size saveFreeSpace)
2320{
2321	size_t		page_avail = BLCKSZ - SizeOfPageHeaderData - saveFreeSpace;
2322	int			npages = 1;
2323
2324	for (int i = done; i < ntuples; i++)
2325	{
2326		size_t		tup_sz = sizeof(ItemIdData) + MAXALIGN(heaptuples[i]->t_len);
2327
2328		if (page_avail < tup_sz)
2329		{
2330			npages++;
2331			page_avail = BLCKSZ - SizeOfPageHeaderData - saveFreeSpace;
2332		}
2333		page_avail -= tup_sz;
2334	}
2335
2336	return npages;
2337}
2338
2339/*
2340 *	heap_multi_insert	- insert multiple tuples into a heap
2341 *
2342 * This is like heap_insert(), but inserts multiple tuples in one operation.
2343 * That's faster than calling heap_insert() in a loop, because when multiple
2344 * tuples can be inserted on a single page, we can write just a single WAL
2345 * record covering all of them, and only need to lock/unlock the page once.
2346 *
2347 * Note: this leaks memory into the current memory context. You can create a
2348 * temporary context before calling this, if that's a problem.
2349 */
2350void
2351heap_multi_insert(Relation relation, TupleTableSlot **slots, int ntuples,
2352				  CommandId cid, int options, BulkInsertState bistate)
2353{
2354	TransactionId xid = GetCurrentTransactionId();
2355	HeapTuple  *heaptuples;
2356	int			i;
2357	int			ndone;
2358	PGAlignedBlock scratch;
2359	Page		page;
2360	Buffer		vmbuffer = InvalidBuffer;
2361	bool		needwal;
2362	Size		saveFreeSpace;
2363	bool		need_tuple_data = RelationIsLogicallyLogged(relation);
2364	bool		need_cids = RelationIsAccessibleInLogicalDecoding(relation);
2365	bool		starting_with_empty_page = false;
2366	int			npages = 0;
2367	int			npages_used = 0;
2368
2369	/* currently not needed (thus unsupported) for heap_multi_insert() */
2370	Assert(!(options & HEAP_INSERT_NO_LOGICAL));
2371
2372	AssertHasSnapshotForToast(relation);
2373
2374	needwal = RelationNeedsWAL(relation);
2375	saveFreeSpace = RelationGetTargetPageFreeSpace(relation,
2376												   HEAP_DEFAULT_FILLFACTOR);
2377
2378	/* Toast and set header data in all the slots */
2379	heaptuples = palloc(ntuples * sizeof(HeapTuple));
2380	for (i = 0; i < ntuples; i++)
2381	{
2382		HeapTuple	tuple;
2383
2384		tuple = ExecFetchSlotHeapTuple(slots[i], true, NULL);
2385		slots[i]->tts_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
2386		tuple->t_tableOid = slots[i]->tts_tableOid;
2387		heaptuples[i] = heap_prepare_insert(relation, tuple, xid, cid,
2388											options);
2389	}
2390
2391	/*
2392	 * We're about to do the actual inserts -- but check for conflict first,
2393	 * to minimize the possibility of having to roll back work we've just
2394	 * done.
2395	 *
2396	 * A check here does not definitively prevent a serialization anomaly;
2397	 * that check MUST be done at least past the point of acquiring an
2398	 * exclusive buffer content lock on every buffer that will be affected,
2399	 * and MAY be done after all inserts are reflected in the buffers and
2400	 * those locks are released; otherwise there is a race condition.  Since
2401	 * multiple buffers can be locked and unlocked in the loop below, and it
2402	 * would not be feasible to identify and lock all of those buffers before
2403	 * the loop, we must do a final check at the end.
2404	 *
2405	 * The check here could be omitted with no loss of correctness; it is
2406	 * present strictly as an optimization.
2407	 *
2408	 * For heap inserts, we only need to check for table-level SSI locks. Our
2409	 * new tuples can't possibly conflict with existing tuple locks, and heap
2410	 * page locks are only consolidated versions of tuple locks; they do not
2411	 * lock "gaps" as index page locks do.  So we don't need to specify a
