2412	 * buffer when making the call, which makes for a faster check.
2413	 */
2414	CheckForSerializableConflictIn(relation, NULL, InvalidBlockNumber);
2415
2416	ndone = 0;
2417	while (ndone < ntuples)
2418	{
2419		Buffer		buffer;
2420		bool		all_visible_cleared = false;
2421		bool		all_frozen_set = false;
2422		int			nthispage;
2423
2424		CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS();
2425
2426		/*
2427		 * Compute number of pages needed to fit the to-be-inserted tuples in
2428		 * the worst case.  This will be used to determine how much to extend
2429		 * the relation by in RelationGetBufferForTuple(), if needed.  If we
2430		 * filled a prior page from scratch, we can just update our last
2431		 * computation, but if we started with a partially filled page,
2432		 * recompute from scratch, the number of potentially required pages
2433		 * can vary due to tuples needing to fit onto the page, page headers
2434		 * etc.
2435		 */
2436		if (ndone == 0 || !starting_with_empty_page)
2437		{
2438			npages = heap_multi_insert_pages(heaptuples, ndone, ntuples,
2439											 saveFreeSpace);
2440			npages_used = 0;
2441		}
2442		else
2443			npages_used++;
2444
2445		/*
2446		 * Find buffer where at least the next tuple will fit.  If the page is
2447		 * all-visible, this will also pin the requisite visibility map page.
2448		 *
2449		 * Also pin visibility map page if COPY FREEZE inserts tuples into an
2450		 * empty page. See all_frozen_set below.
2451		 */
2452		buffer = RelationGetBufferForTuple(relation, heaptuples[ndone]->t_len,
2453										   InvalidBuffer, options, bistate,
2454										   &vmbuffer, NULL,
2455										   npages - npages_used);
2456		page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
2457
2458		starting_with_empty_page = PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) == 0;
2459
2460		if (starting_with_empty_page && (options & HEAP_INSERT_FROZEN))
2461			all_frozen_set = true;
2462
2463		/* NO EREPORT(ERROR) from here till changes are logged */
2464		START_CRIT_SECTION();
2465
2466		/*
2467		 * RelationGetBufferForTuple has ensured that the first tuple fits.
2468		 * Put that on the page, and then as many other tuples as fit.
2469		 */
2470		RelationPutHeapTuple(relation, buffer, heaptuples[ndone], false);
2471
2472		/*
2473		 * For logical decoding we need combo CIDs to properly decode the
2474		 * catalog.
2475		 */
2476		if (needwal && need_cids)
2477			log_heap_new_cid(relation, heaptuples[ndone]);
2478
2479		for (nthispage = 1; ndone + nthispage < ntuples; nthispage++)
2480		{
2481			HeapTuple	heaptup = heaptuples[ndone + nthispage];
2482
2483			if (PageGetHeapFreeSpace(page) < MAXALIGN(heaptup->t_len) + saveFreeSpace)
2484				break;
2485
2486			RelationPutHeapTuple(relation, buffer, heaptup, false);
2487
2488			/*
2489			 * For logical decoding we need combo CIDs to properly decode the
2490			 * catalog.
2491			 */
2492			if (needwal && need_cids)
2493				log_heap_new_cid(relation, heaptup);
2494		}
2495
2496		/*
2497		 * If the page is all visible, need to clear that, unless we're only
2498		 * going to add further frozen rows to it.
2499		 *
2500		 * If we're only adding already frozen rows to a previously empty
2501		 * page, mark it as all-visible.
2502		 */
2503		if (PageIsAllVisible(page) && !(options & HEAP_INSERT_FROZEN))
2504		{
2505			all_visible_cleared = true;
2506			PageClearAllVisible(page);
2507			visibilitymap_clear(relation,
2508								BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer),
2509								vmbuffer, VISIBILITYMAP_VALID_BITS);
2510		}
2511		else if (all_frozen_set)
2512			PageSetAllVisible(page);
2513
2514		/*
2515		 * XXX Should we set PageSetPrunable on this page ? See heap_insert()
2516		 */
2517
2518		MarkBufferDirty(buffer);
2519
2520		/* XLOG stuff */
2521		if (needwal)
2522		{
2523			XLogRecPtr	recptr;
2524			xl_heap_multi_insert *xlrec;
2525			uint8		info = XLOG_HEAP2_MULTI_INSERT;
2526			char	   *tupledata;
2527			int			totaldatalen;
2528			char	   *scratchptr = scratch.data;
2529			bool		init;
2530			int			bufflags = 0;
2531
2532			/*
2533			 * If the page was previously empty, we can reinit the page
2534			 * instead of restoring the whole thing.
2535			 */
2536			init = starting_with_empty_page;
2537
2538			/* allocate xl_heap_multi_insert struct from the scratch area */
2539			xlrec = (xl_heap_multi_insert *) scratchptr;
2540			scratchptr += SizeOfHeapMultiInsert;
2541
2542			/*
2543			 * Allocate offsets array. Unless we're reinitializing the page,
2544			 * in that case the tuples are stored in order starting at
2545			 * FirstOffsetNumber and we don't need to store the offsets
2546			 * explicitly.
2547			 */
2548			if (!init)
2549				scratchptr += nthispage * sizeof(OffsetNumber);
2550
2551			/* the rest of the scratch space is used for tuple data */
2552			tupledata = scratchptr;
2553
2554			/* check that the mutually exclusive flags are not both set */
2555			Assert(!(all_visible_cleared && all_frozen_set));
2556
2557			xlrec->flags = 0;
2558			if (all_visible_cleared)
2559				xlrec->flags = XLH_INSERT_ALL_VISIBLE_CLEARED;
2560			if (all_frozen_set)
2561				xlrec->flags = XLH_INSERT_ALL_FROZEN_SET;
2562
2563			xlrec->ntuples = nthispage;
2564
2565			/*
2566			 * Write out an xl_multi_insert_tuple and the tuple data itself
2567			 * for each tuple.
2568			 */
2569			for (i = 0; i < nthispage; i++)
2570			{
2571				HeapTuple	heaptup = heaptuples[ndone + i];
2572				xl_multi_insert_tuple *tuphdr;
2573				int			datalen;
2574
2575				if (!init)
2576					xlrec->offsets[i] = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&heaptup->t_self);
2577				/* xl_multi_insert_tuple needs two-byte alignment. */
2578				tuphdr = (xl_multi_insert_tuple *) SHORTALIGN(scratchptr);
2579				scratchptr = ((char *) tuphdr) + SizeOfMultiInsertTuple;
2580
2581				tuphdr->t_infomask2 = heaptup->t_data->t_infomask2;
2582				tuphdr->t_infomask = heaptup->t_data->t_infomask;
2583				tuphdr->t_hoff = heaptup->t_data->t_hoff;
2584
2585				/* write bitmap [+ padding] [+ oid] + data */
2586				datalen = heaptup->t_len - SizeofHeapTupleHeader;
2587				memcpy(scratchptr,
2588					   (char *) heaptup->t_data + SizeofHeapTupleHeader,
2589					   datalen);
2590				tuphdr->datalen = datalen;
2591				scratchptr += datalen;
2592			}
2593			totaldatalen = scratchptr - tupledata;
2594			Assert((scratchptr - scratch.data) < BLCKSZ);
2595
2596			if (need_tuple_data)
2597				xlrec->flags |= XLH_INSERT_CONTAINS_NEW_TUPLE;
2598
2599			/*
2600			 * Signal that this is the last xl_heap_multi_insert record
2601			 * emitted by this call to heap_multi_insert(). Needed for logical
2602			 * decoding so it knows when to cleanup temporary data.
2603			 */
2604			if (ndone + nthispage == ntuples)
2605				xlrec->flags |= XLH_INSERT_LAST_IN_MULTI;
2606
2607			if (init)
2608			{
2609				info |= XLOG_HEAP_INIT_PAGE;
2610				bufflags |= REGBUF_WILL_INIT;
2611			}
2612
2613			/*
2614			 * If we're doing logical decoding, include the new tuple data
2615			 * even if we take a full-page image of the page.
2616			 */
2617			if (need_tuple_data)
2618				bufflags |= REGBUF_KEEP_DATA;
2619
2620			XLogBeginInsert();
2621			XLogRegisterData(xlrec, tupledata - scratch.data);
2622			XLogRegisterBuffer(0, buffer, REGBUF_STANDARD | bufflags);
2623
2624			XLogRegisterBufData(0, tupledata, totaldatalen);
2625
2626			/* filtering by origin on a row level is much more efficient */
2627			XLogSetRecordFlags(XLOG_INCLUDE_ORIGIN);
2628
2629			recptr = XLogInsert(RM_HEAP2_ID, info);
2630
2631			PageSetLSN(page, recptr);
2632		}
2633
2634		END_CRIT_SECTION();
2635
2636		/*
2637		 * If we've frozen everything on the page, update the visibilitymap.
2638		 * We're already holding pin on the vmbuffer.
2639		 */
2640		if (all_frozen_set)
2641		{
2642			Assert(PageIsAllVisible(page));
2643			Assert(visibilitymap_pin_ok(BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer), vmbuffer));
2644
2645			/*
2646			 * It's fine to use InvalidTransactionId here - this is only used
2647			 * when HEAP_INSERT_FROZEN is specified, which intentionally
2648			 * violates visibility rules.
2649			 */
2650			visibilitymap_set(relation, BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer), buffer,
2651							  InvalidXLogRecPtr, vmbuffer,
2652							  InvalidTransactionId,
2653							  VISIBILITYMAP_ALL_VISIBLE | VISIBILITYMAP_ALL_FROZEN);
2654		}
2655
2656		UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
2657		ndone += nthispage;
2658
2659		/*
2660		 * NB: Only release vmbuffer after inserting all tuples - it's fairly
2661		 * likely that we'll insert into subsequent heap pages that are likely
2662		 * to use the same vm page.
2663		 */
2664	}
2665
2666	/* We're done with inserting all tuples, so release the last vmbuffer. */
2667	if (vmbuffer != InvalidBuffer)
2668		ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
2669
2670	/*
2671	 * We're done with the actual inserts.  Check for conflicts again, to
2672	 * ensure that all rw-conflicts in to these inserts are detected.  Without
2673	 * this final check, a sequential scan of the heap may have locked the
2674	 * table after the "before" check, missing one opportunity to detect the
2675	 * conflict, and then scanned the table before the new tuples were there,
2676	 * missing the other chance to detect the conflict.
2677	 *
2678	 * For heap inserts, we only need to check for table-level SSI locks. Our
2679	 * new tuples can't possibly conflict with existing tuple locks, and heap
2680	 * page locks are only consolidated versions of tuple locks; they do not
2681	 * lock "gaps" as index page locks do.  So we don't need to specify a
2682	 * buffer when making the call.
2683	 */
2684	CheckForSerializableConflictIn(relation, NULL, InvalidBlockNumber);
2685
2686	/*
2687	 * If tuples are cachable, mark them for invalidation from the caches in
2688	 * case we abort.  Note it is OK to do this after releasing the buffer,
2689	 * because the heaptuples data structure is all in local memory, not in
2690	 * the shared buffer.
2691	 */
2692	if (IsCatalogRelation(relation))
2693	{
2694		for (i = 0; i < ntuples; i++)
2695			CacheInvalidateHeapTuple(relation, heaptuples[i], NULL);
2696	}
2697
2698	/* copy t_self fields back to the caller's slots */
2699	for (i = 0; i < ntuples; i++)
2700		slots[i]->tts_tid = heaptuples[i]->t_self;
2701
2702	pgstat_count_heap_insert(relation, ntuples);
2703}
2704
2705/*
2706 *	simple_heap_insert - insert a tuple
2707 *
2708 * Currently, this routine differs from heap_insert only in supplying
2709 * a default command ID and not allowing access to the speedup options.
2710 *
2711 * This should be used rather than using heap_insert directly in most places
2712 * where we are modifying system catalogs.
2713 */
2714void
2715simple_heap_insert(Relation relation, HeapTuple tup)
2716{
2717	heap_insert(relation, tup, GetCurrentCommandId(true), 0, NULL);
2718}
2719
2720/*
2721 * Given infomask/infomask2, compute the bits that must be saved in the
2722 * "infobits" field of xl_heap_delete, xl_heap_update, xl_heap_lock,
2723 * xl_heap_lock_updated WAL records.
2724 *
2725 * See fix_infomask_from_infobits.
2726 */
2727static uint8
2728compute_infobits(uint16 infomask, uint16 infomask2)
2729{
2730	return
2731		((infomask & HEAP_XMAX_IS_MULTI) != 0 ? XLHL_XMAX_IS_MULTI : 0) |
2732		((infomask & HEAP_XMAX_LOCK_ONLY) != 0 ? XLHL_XMAX_LOCK_ONLY : 0) |
2733		((infomask & HEAP_XMAX_EXCL_LOCK) != 0 ? XLHL_XMAX_EXCL_LOCK : 0) |
2734	/* note we ignore HEAP_XMAX_SHR_LOCK here */
2735		((infomask & HEAP_XMAX_KEYSHR_LOCK) != 0 ? XLHL_XMAX_KEYSHR_LOCK : 0) |
2736		((infomask2 & HEAP_KEYS_UPDATED) != 0 ?
2737		 XLHL_KEYS_UPDATED : 0);
2738}
2739
2740/*
2741 * Given two versions of the same t_infomask for a tuple, compare them and
2742 * return whether the relevant status for a tuple Xmax has changed.  This is
2743 * used after a buffer lock has been released and reacquired: we want to ensure
2744 * that the tuple state continues to be the same it was when we previously
2745 * examined it.
2746 *
2747 * Note the Xmax field itself must be compared separately.
2748 */
2749static inline bool
2750xmax_infomask_changed(uint16 new_infomask, uint16 old_infomask)
2751{
2752	const uint16 interesting =
2753		HEAP_XMAX_IS_MULTI | HEAP_XMAX_LOCK_ONLY | HEAP_LOCK_MASK;
2754
2755	if ((new_infomask & interesting) != (old_infomask & interesting))
2756		return true;
2757
2758	return false;
2759}
2760
2761/*
2762 *	heap_delete - delete a tuple
2763 *
2764 * See table_tuple_delete() for an explanation of the parameters, except that
2765 * this routine directly takes a tuple rather than a slot.
2766 *
2767 * In the failure cases, the routine fills *tmfd with the tuple's t_ctid,
2768 * t_xmax (resolving a possible MultiXact, if necessary), and t_cmax (the last
2769 * only for TM_SelfModified, since we cannot obtain cmax from a combo CID
2770 * generated by another transaction).
2771 */
2772TM_Result
2773heap_delete(Relation relation, ItemPointer tid,
2774			CommandId cid, Snapshot crosscheck, bool wait,
2775			TM_FailureData *tmfd, bool changingPart)
2776{
2777	TM_Result	result;
2778	TransactionId xid = GetCurrentTransactionId();
2779	ItemId		lp;
2780	HeapTupleData tp;
2781	Page		page;
2782	BlockNumber block;
2783	Buffer		buffer;
2784	Buffer		vmbuffer = InvalidBuffer;
2785	TransactionId new_xmax;
2786	uint16		new_infomask,
2787				new_infomask2;
2788	bool		have_tuple_lock = false;
2789	bool		iscombo;
2790	bool		all_visible_cleared = false;
2791	HeapTuple	old_key_tuple = NULL;	/* replica identity of the tuple */
2792	bool		old_key_copied = false;
2793
2794	Assert(ItemPointerIsValid(tid));
2795
2796	AssertHasSnapshotForToast(relation);
2797
2798	/*
2799	 * Forbid this during a parallel operation, lest it allocate a combo CID.
2800	 * Other workers might need that combo CID for visibility checks, and we
2801	 * have no provision for broadcasting it to them.
2802	 */
2803	if (IsInParallelMode())
2804		ereport(ERROR,
2805				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TRANSACTION_STATE),
2806				 errmsg("cannot delete tuples during a parallel operation")));
2807
2808	block = ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid);
2809	buffer = ReadBuffer(relation, block);
2810	page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
2811
2812	/*
2813	 * Before locking the buffer, pin the visibility map page if it appears to
2814	 * be necessary.  Since we haven't got the lock yet, someone else might be
2815	 * in the middle of changing this, so we'll need to recheck after we have
2816	 * the lock.
2817	 */
2818	if (PageIsAllVisible(page))
2819		visibilitymap_pin(relation, block, &vmbuffer);
2820
2821	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
2822
2823	lp = PageGetItemId(page, ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(tid));
2824	Assert(ItemIdIsNormal(lp));
2825
2826	tp.t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
2827	tp.t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lp);
2828	tp.t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lp);
2829	tp.t_self = *tid;
2830
2831l1:
2832
2833	/*
2834	 * If we didn't pin the visibility map page and the page has become all
2835	 * visible while we were busy locking the buffer, we'll have to unlock and
2836	 * re-lock, to avoid holding the buffer lock across an I/O.  That's a bit
2837	 * unfortunate, but hopefully shouldn't happen often.
2838	 */
2839	if (vmbuffer == InvalidBuffer && PageIsAllVisible(page))
2840	{
2841		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
2842		visibilitymap_pin(relation, block, &vmbuffer);
2843		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
2844	}
2845
2846	result = HeapTupleSatisfiesUpdate(&tp, cid, buffer);
2847
2848	if (result == TM_Invisible)
2849	{
2850		UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
2851		ereport(ERROR,
2852				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
2853				 errmsg("attempted to delete invisible tuple")));
2854	}
2855	else if (result == TM_BeingModified && wait)
2856	{
2857		TransactionId xwait;
2858		uint16		infomask;
2859
2860		/* must copy state data before unlocking buffer */
2861		xwait = HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(tp.t_data);
2862		infomask = tp.t_data->t_infomask;
2863
2864		/*
2865		 * Sleep until concurrent transaction ends -- except when there's a
2866		 * single locker and it's our own transaction.  Note we don't care
2867		 * which lock mode the locker has, because we need the strongest one.
2868		 *
2869		 * Before sleeping, we need to acquire tuple lock to establish our
2870		 * priority for the tuple (see heap_lock_tuple).  LockTuple will
2871		 * release us when we are next-in-line for the tuple.
2872		 *
2873		 * If we are forced to "start over" below, we keep the tuple lock;
2874		 * this arranges that we stay at the head of the line while rechecking
2875		 * tuple state.
2876		 */
2877		if (infomask & HEAP_XMAX_IS_MULTI)
2878		{
2879			bool		current_is_member = false;
2880
2881			if (DoesMultiXactIdConflict((MultiXactId) xwait, infomask,
2882										LockTupleExclusive, &current_is_member))
2883			{
2884				LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
2885
2886				/*
2887				 * Acquire the lock, if necessary (but skip it when we're
2888				 * requesting a lock and already have one; avoids deadlock).
2889				 */
2890				if (!current_is_member)
2891					heap_acquire_tuplock(relation, &(tp.t_self), LockTupleExclusive,
2892										 LockWaitBlock, &have_tuple_lock);
2893
2894				/* wait for multixact */
2895				MultiXactIdWait((MultiXactId) xwait, MultiXactStatusUpdate, infomask,
2896								relation, &(tp.t_self), XLTW_Delete,
2897								NULL);
2898				LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
2899
2900				/*
2901				 * If xwait had just locked the tuple then some other xact
2902				 * could update this tuple before we get to this point.  Check
2903				 * for xmax change, and start over if so.
2904				 *
2905				 * We also must start over if we didn't pin the VM page, and
2906				 * the page has become all visible.
2907				 */
2908				if ((vmbuffer == InvalidBuffer && PageIsAllVisible(page)) ||
2909					xmax_infomask_changed(tp.t_data->t_infomask, infomask) ||
2910					!TransactionIdEquals(HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(tp.t_data),
2911										 xwait))
2912					goto l1;
2913			}
2914
2915			/*
2916			 * You might think the multixact is necessarily done here, but not
2917			 * so: it could have surviving members, namely our own xact or
2918			 * other subxacts of this backend.  It is legal for us to delete
2919			 * the tuple in either case, however (the latter case is
2920			 * essentially a situation of upgrading our former shared lock to
2921			 * exclusive).  We don't bother changing the on-disk hint bits
2922			 * since we are about to overwrite the xmax altogether.
2923			 */
2924		}
2925		else if (!TransactionIdIsCurrentTransactionId(xwait))
2926		{
2927			/*
2928			 * Wait for regular transaction to end; but first, acquire tuple
2929			 * lock.
2930			 */
2931			LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
2932			heap_acquire_tuplock(relation, &(tp.t_self), LockTupleExclusive,
2933								 LockWaitBlock, &have_tuple_lock);
2934			XactLockTableWait(xwait, relation, &(tp.t_self), XLTW_Delete);
2935			LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
2936
2937			/*
2938			 * xwait is done, but if xwait had just locked the tuple then some
2939			 * other xact could update this tuple before we get to this point.
2940			 * Check for xmax change, and start over if so.
2941			 *
2942			 * We also must start over if we didn't pin the VM page, and the
2943			 * page has become all visible.
2944			 */
2945			if ((vmbuffer == InvalidBuffer && PageIsAllVisible(page)) ||
2946				xmax_infomask_changed(tp.t_data->t_infomask, infomask) ||
2947				!TransactionIdEquals(HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(tp.t_data),
2948									 xwait))
2949				goto l1;
2950
2951			/* Otherwise check if it committed or aborted */
2952			UpdateXmaxHintBits(tp.t_data, buffer, xwait);
2953		}
2954
2955		/*
2956		 * We may overwrite if previous xmax aborted, or if it committed but
2957		 * only locked the tuple without updating it.
2958		 */
2959		if ((tp.t_data->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_INVALID) ||
2960			HEAP_XMAX_IS_LOCKED_ONLY(tp.t_data->t_infomask) ||
2961			HeapTupleHeaderIsOnlyLocked(tp.t_data))
2962			result = TM_Ok;
2963		else if (!ItemPointerEquals(&tp.t_self, &tp.t_data->t_ctid))
2964			result = TM_Updated;
2965		else
2966			result = TM_Deleted;
2967	}
2968
2969	/* sanity check the result HeapTupleSatisfiesUpdate() and the logic above */
2970	if (result != TM_Ok)
2971	{
2972		Assert(result == TM_SelfModified ||
2973			   result == TM_Updated ||
2974			   result == TM_Deleted ||
2975			   result == TM_BeingModified);
2976		Assert(!(tp.t_data->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_INVALID));
2977		Assert(result != TM_Updated ||
2978			   !ItemPointerEquals(&tp.t_self, &tp.t_data->t_ctid));
2979	}
2980
2981	if (crosscheck != InvalidSnapshot && result == TM_Ok)
2982	{
2983		/* Perform additional check for transaction-snapshot mode RI updates */
2984		if (!HeapTupleSatisfiesVisibility(&tp, crosscheck, buffer))
2985			result = TM_Updated;
2986	}
2987
2988	if (result != TM_Ok)
2989	{
2990		tmfd->ctid = tp.t_data->t_ctid;
2991		tmfd->xmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetUpdateXid(tp.t_data);
2992		if (result == TM_SelfModified)
2993			tmfd->cmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetCmax(tp.t_data);
2994		else
2995			tmfd->cmax = InvalidCommandId;
2996		UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
2997		if (have_tuple_lock)
2998			UnlockTupleTuplock(relation, &(tp.t_self), LockTupleExclusive);
2999		if (vmbuffer != InvalidBuffer)
3000			ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
3001		return result;
3002	}
3003
3004	/*
3005	 * We're about to do the actual delete -- check for conflict first, to
3006	 * avoid possibly having to roll back work we've just done.
3007	 *
3008	 * This is safe without a recheck as long as there is no possibility of
3009	 * another process scanning the page between this check and the delete
3010	 * being visible to the scan (i.e., an exclusive buffer content lock is
3011	 * continuously held from this point until the tuple delete is visible).
3012	 */
3013	CheckForSerializableConflictIn(relation, tid, BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer));
3014
3015	/* replace cid with a combo CID if necessary */
3016	HeapTupleHeaderAdjustCmax(tp.t_data, &cid, &iscombo);
3017
3018	/*
3019	 * Compute replica identity tuple before entering the critical section so
3020	 * we don't PANIC upon a memory allocation failure.
3021	 */
3022	old_key_tuple = ExtractReplicaIdentity(relation, &tp, true, &old_key_copied);
3023
3024	/*
3025	 * If this is the first possibly-multixact-able operation in the current
3026	 * transaction, set my per-backend OldestMemberMXactId setting. We can be
3027	 * certain that the transaction will never become a member of any older
3028	 * MultiXactIds than that.  (We have to do this even if we end up just
3029	 * using our own TransactionId below, since some other backend could
3030	 * incorporate our XID into a MultiXact immediately afterwards.)
3031	 */
3032	MultiXactIdSetOldestMember();
3033
3034	compute_new_xmax_infomask(HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(tp.t_data),
3035							  tp.t_data->t_infomask, tp.t_data->t_infomask2,
3036							  xid, LockTupleExclusive, true,
3037							  &new_xmax, &new_infomask, &new_infomask2);
3038
3039	START_CRIT_SECTION();
3040
3041	/*
3042	 * If this transaction commits, the tuple will become DEAD sooner or
3043	 * later.  Set flag that this page is a candidate for pruning once our xid
3044	 * falls below the OldestXmin horizon.  If the transaction finally aborts,
3045	 * the subsequent page pruning will be a no-op and the hint will be
3046	 * cleared.
3047	 */
3048	PageSetPrunable(page, xid);
3049
3050	if (PageIsAllVisible(page))
3051	{
3052		all_visible_cleared = true;
3053		PageClearAllVisible(page);
3054		visibilitymap_clear(relation, BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer),
3055							vmbuffer, VISIBILITYMAP_VALID_BITS);
3056	}
3057
3058	/* store transaction information of xact deleting the tuple */
3059	tp.t_data->t_infomask &= ~(HEAP_XMAX_BITS | HEAP_MOVED);
3060	tp.t_data->t_infomask2 &= ~HEAP_KEYS_UPDATED;
3061	tp.t_data->t_infomask |= new_infomask;
3062	tp.t_data->t_infomask2 |= new_infomask2;
3063	HeapTupleHeaderClearHotUpdated(tp.t_data);
3064	HeapTupleHeaderSetXmax(tp.t_data, new_xmax);
3065	HeapTupleHeaderSetCmax(tp.t_data, cid, iscombo);
3066	/* Make sure there is no forward chain link in t_ctid */
3067	tp.t_data->t_ctid = tp.t_self;
3068
3069	/* Signal that this is actually a move into another partition */
3070	if (changingPart)
3071		HeapTupleHeaderSetMovedPartitions(tp.t_data);
3072
3073	MarkBufferDirty(buffer);
3074
3075	/*
3076	 * XLOG stuff
3077	 *
3078	 * NB: heap_abort_speculative() uses the same xlog record and replay
3079	 * routines.
3080	 */
3081	if (RelationNeedsWAL(relation))
3082	{
3083		xl_heap_delete xlrec;
3084		xl_heap_header xlhdr;
3085		XLogRecPtr	recptr;
3086
3087		/*
3088		 * For logical decode we need combo CIDs to properly decode the
3089		 * catalog
3090		 */
3091		if (RelationIsAccessibleInLogicalDecoding(relation))
3092			log_heap_new_cid(relation, &tp);
3093
3094		xlrec.flags = 0;
3095		if (all_visible_cleared)
3096			xlrec.flags |= XLH_DELETE_ALL_VISIBLE_CLEARED;
3097		if (changingPart)
3098			xlrec.flags |= XLH_DELETE_IS_PARTITION_MOVE;
3099		xlrec.infobits_set = compute_infobits(tp.t_data->t_infomask,
3100											  tp.t_data->t_infomask2);
3101		xlrec.offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&tp.t_self);
3102		xlrec.xmax = new_xmax;
3103
3104		if (old_key_tuple != NULL)
3105		{
3106			if (relation->rd_rel->relreplident == REPLICA_IDENTITY_FULL)
3107				xlrec.flags |= XLH_DELETE_CONTAINS_OLD_TUPLE;
3108			else
3109				xlrec.flags |= XLH_DELETE_CONTAINS_OLD_KEY;
3110		}
3111
3112		XLogBeginInsert();
3113		XLogRegisterData(&xlrec, SizeOfHeapDelete);
3114
3115		XLogRegisterBuffer(0, buffer, REGBUF_STANDARD);
3116
3117		/*
3118		 * Log replica identity of the deleted tuple if there is one
3119		 */
3120		if (old_key_tuple != NULL)
3121		{
3122			xlhdr.t_infomask2 = old_key_tuple->t_data->t_infomask2;
3123			xlhdr.t_infomask = old_key_tuple->t_data->t_infomask;
3124			xlhdr.t_hoff = old_key_tuple->t_data->t_hoff;
3125
3126			XLogRegisterData(&xlhdr, SizeOfHeapHeader);
3127			XLogRegisterData((char *) old_key_tuple->t_data
3128							 + SizeofHeapTupleHeader,
3129							 old_key_tuple->t_len
3130							 - SizeofHeapTupleHeader);
3131		}
3132
3133		/* filtering by origin on a row level is much more efficient */
3134		XLogSetRecordFlags(XLOG_INCLUDE_ORIGIN);
3135
3136		recptr = XLogInsert(RM_HEAP_ID, XLOG_HEAP_DELETE);
3137
3138		PageSetLSN(page, recptr);
3139	}
3140
3141	END_CRIT_SECTION();
3142
3143	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
3144
3145	if (vmbuffer != InvalidBuffer)
3146		ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
3147
3148	/*
3149	 * If the tuple has toasted out-of-line attributes, we need to delete
3150	 * those items too.  We have to do this before releasing the buffer
3151	 * because we need to look at the contents of the tuple, but it's OK to
3152	 * release the content lock on the buffer first.
3153	 */
3154	if (relation->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
3155		relation->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_MATVIEW)
3156	{
3157		/* toast table entries should never be recursively toasted */
3158		Assert(!HeapTupleHasExternal(&tp));
3159	}
3160	else if (HeapTupleHasExternal(&tp))
3161		heap_toast_delete(relation, &tp, false);
3162
3163	/*
3164	 * Mark tuple for invalidation from system caches at next command
3165	 * boundary. We have to do this before releasing the buffer because we
3166	 * need to look at the contents of the tuple.
3167	 */
3168	CacheInvalidateHeapTuple(relation, &tp, NULL);
3169
3170	/* Now we can release the buffer */
3171	ReleaseBuffer(buffer);
3172
3173	/*
3174	 * Release the lmgr tuple lock, if we had it.
3175	 */
3176	if (have_tuple_lock)
3177		UnlockTupleTuplock(relation, &(tp.t_self), LockTupleExclusive);
3178
3179	pgstat_count_heap_delete(relation);
3180
3181	if (old_key_tuple != NULL && old_key_copied)
3182		heap_freetuple(old_key_tuple);
3183
3184	return TM_Ok;
3185}
3186
3187/*
3188 *	simple_heap_delete - delete a tuple
3189 *
3190 * This routine may be used to delete a tuple when concurrent updates of
3191 * the target tuple are not expected (for example, because we have a lock
3192 * on the relation associated with the tuple).  Any failure is reported
3193 * via ereport().
3194 */
3195void
3196simple_heap_delete(Relation relation, ItemPointer tid)
3197{
3198	TM_Result	result;
3199	TM_FailureData tmfd;
3200
3201	result = heap_delete(relation, tid,
3202						 GetCurrentCommandId(true), InvalidSnapshot,
3203						 true /* wait for commit */ ,
3204						 &tmfd, false /* changingPart */ );
3205	switch (result)
3206	{
3207		case TM_SelfModified:
3208			/* Tuple was already updated in current command? */
3209			elog(ERROR, "tuple already updated by self");
3210			break;
3211
3212		case TM_Ok:
3213			/* done successfully */
3214			break;
3215
3216		case TM_Updated:
3217			elog(ERROR, "tuple concurrently updated");
3218			break;
3219
3220		case TM_Deleted:
3221			elog(ERROR, "tuple concurrently deleted");
3222			break;
3223
3224		default:
3225			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized heap_delete status: %u", result);
3226			break;
3227	}
3228}
3229
3230/*
3231 *	heap_update - replace a tuple
3232 *
3233 * See table_tuple_update() for an explanation of the parameters, except that
3234 * this routine directly takes a tuple rather than a slot.
3235 *
3236 * In the failure cases, the routine fills *tmfd with the tuple's t_ctid,
3237 * t_xmax (resolving a possible MultiXact, if necessary), and t_cmax (the last
3238 * only for TM_SelfModified, since we cannot obtain cmax from a combo CID
3239 * generated by another transaction).
3240 */
3241TM_Result
3242heap_update(Relation relation, ItemPointer otid, HeapTuple newtup,
3243			CommandId cid, Snapshot crosscheck, bool wait,
3244			TM_FailureData *tmfd, LockTupleMode *lockmode,
3245			TU_UpdateIndexes *update_indexes)
3246{
3247	TM_Result	result;
3248	TransactionId xid = GetCurrentTransactionId();
3249	Bitmapset  *hot_attrs;
3250	Bitmapset  *sum_attrs;
3251	Bitmapset  *key_attrs;
3252	Bitmapset  *id_attrs;
3253	Bitmapset  *interesting_attrs;
3254	Bitmapset  *modified_attrs;
3255	ItemId		lp;
3256	HeapTupleData oldtup;
3257	HeapTuple	heaptup;
3258	HeapTuple	old_key_tuple = NULL;
3259	bool		old_key_copied = false;
3260	Page		page;
3261	BlockNumber block;
3262	MultiXactStatus mxact_status;
3263	Buffer		buffer,
3264				newbuf,
3265				vmbuffer = InvalidBuffer,
3266				vmbuffer_new = InvalidBuffer;
3267	bool		need_toast;
3268	Size		newtupsize,
3269				pagefree;
3270	bool		have_tuple_lock = false;
3271	bool		iscombo;
3272	bool		use_hot_update = false;
3273	bool		summarized_update = false;
3274	bool		key_intact;
3275	bool		all_visible_cleared = false;
3276	bool		all_visible_cleared_new = false;
3277	bool		checked_lockers;
3278	bool		locker_remains;
3279	bool		id_has_external = false;
3280	TransactionId xmax_new_tuple,
3281				xmax_old_tuple;
3282	uint16		infomask_old_tuple,
3283				infomask2_old_tuple,
3284				infomask_new_tuple,
3285				infomask2_new_tuple;
3286
3287	Assert(ItemPointerIsValid(otid));
3288
3289	/* Cheap, simplistic check that the tuple matches the rel's rowtype. */
3290	Assert(HeapTupleHeaderGetNatts(newtup->t_data) <=
3291		   RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(relation));
3292
3293	AssertHasSnapshotForToast(relation);
3294
3295	/*
3296	 * Forbid this during a parallel operation, lest it allocate a combo CID.
3297	 * Other workers might need that combo CID for visibility checks, and we
3298	 * have no provision for broadcasting it to them.
3299	 */
3300	if (IsInParallelMode())
3301		ereport(ERROR,
3302				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_TRANSACTION_STATE),
3303				 errmsg("cannot update tuples during a parallel operation")));
3304
3305#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
3306	check_lock_if_inplace_updateable_rel(relation, otid, newtup);
3307#endif
3308
3309	/*
3310	 * Fetch the list of attributes to be checked for various operations.
3311	 *
3312	 * For HOT considerations, this is wasted effort if we fail to update or
3313	 * have to put the new tuple on a different page.  But we must compute the
3314	 * list before obtaining buffer lock --- in the worst case, if we are
3315	 * doing an update on one of the relevant system catalogs, we could
3316	 * deadlock if we try to fetch the list later.  In any case, the relcache
3317	 * caches the data so this is usually pretty cheap.
3318	 *
3319	 * We also need columns used by the replica identity and columns that are
3320	 * considered the "key" of rows in the table.
3321	 *
3322	 * Note that we get copies of each bitmap, so we need not worry about
3323	 * relcache flush happening midway through.
3324	 */
3325	hot_attrs = RelationGetIndexAttrBitmap(relation,
3326										   INDEX_ATTR_BITMAP_HOT_BLOCKING);
3327	sum_attrs = RelationGetIndexAttrBitmap(relation,
3328										   INDEX_ATTR_BITMAP_SUMMARIZED);
3329	key_attrs = RelationGetIndexAttrBitmap(relation, INDEX_ATTR_BITMAP_KEY);
3330	id_attrs = RelationGetIndexAttrBitmap(relation,
3331										  INDEX_ATTR_BITMAP_IDENTITY_KEY);
3332	interesting_attrs = NULL;
3333	interesting_attrs = bms_add_members(interesting_attrs, hot_attrs);
3334	interesting_attrs = bms_add_members(interesting_attrs, sum_attrs);
3335	interesting_attrs = bms_add_members(interesting_attrs, key_attrs);
3336	interesting_attrs = bms_add_members(interesting_attrs, id_attrs);
3337
3338	block = ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(otid);
3339	INJECTION_POINT("heap_update-before-pin", NULL);
3340	buffer = ReadBuffer(relation, block);
3341	page = BufferGetPage(buffer);
3342
3343	/*
3344	 * Before locking the buffer, pin the visibility map page if it appears to
3345	 * be necessary.  Since we haven't got the lock yet, someone else might be
3346	 * in the middle of changing this, so we'll need to recheck after we have
3347	 * the lock.
3348	 */
3349	if (PageIsAllVisible(page))
3350		visibilitymap_pin(relation, block, &vmbuffer);
3351
3352	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
3353
3354	lp = PageGetItemId(page, ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(otid));
3355
3356	/*
3357	 * Usually, a buffer pin and/or snapshot blocks pruning of otid, ensuring
3358	 * we see LP_NORMAL here.  When the otid origin is a syscache, we may have
3359	 * neither a pin nor a snapshot.  Hence, we may see other LP_ states, each
3360	 * of which indicates concurrent pruning.
3361	 *
3362	 * Failing with TM_Updated would be most accurate.  However, unlike other
3363	 * TM_Updated scenarios, we don't know the successor ctid in LP_UNUSED and
3364	 * LP_DEAD cases.  While the distinction between TM_Updated and TM_Deleted
3365	 * does matter to SQL statements UPDATE and MERGE, those SQL statements
3366	 * hold a snapshot that ensures LP_NORMAL.  Hence, the choice between
3367	 * TM_Updated and TM_Deleted affects only the wording of error messages.
3368	 * Settle on TM_Deleted, for two reasons.  First, it avoids complicating
3369	 * the specification of when tmfd->ctid is valid.  Second, it creates
3370	 * error log evidence that we took this branch.
3371	 *
3372	 * Since it's possible to see LP_UNUSED at otid, it's also possible to see
3373	 * LP_NORMAL for a tuple that replaced LP_UNUSED.  If it's a tuple for an
3374	 * unrelated row, we'll fail with "duplicate key value violates unique".
3375	 * XXX if otid is the live, newer version of the newtup row, we'll discard
3376	 * changes originating in versions of this catalog row after the version
3377	 * the caller got from syscache.  See syscache-update-pruned.spec.
3378	 */
3379	if (!ItemIdIsNormal(lp))
3380	{
3381		Assert(RelationSupportsSysCache(RelationGetRelid(relation)));
3382
3383		UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
3384		Assert(!have_tuple_lock);
3385		if (vmbuffer != InvalidBuffer)
3386			ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
3387		tmfd->ctid = *otid;
3388		tmfd->xmax = InvalidTransactionId;
3389		tmfd->cmax = InvalidCommandId;
3390		*update_indexes = TU_None;
3391
3392		bms_free(hot_attrs);
3393		bms_free(sum_attrs);
3394		bms_free(key_attrs);
3395		bms_free(id_attrs);
3396		/* modified_attrs not yet initialized */
3397		bms_free(interesting_attrs);
3398		return TM_Deleted;
3399	}
3400
3401	/*
3402	 * Fill in enough data in oldtup for HeapDetermineColumnsInfo to work
3403	 * properly.
3404	 */
3405	oldtup.t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
3406	oldtup.t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lp);
3407	oldtup.t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lp);
3408	oldtup.t_self = *otid;
3409
3410	/* the new tuple is ready, except for this: */
3411	newtup->t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
3412
3413	/*
3414	 * Determine columns modified by the update.  Additionally, identify
3415	 * whether any of the unmodified replica identity key attributes in the
3416	 * old tuple is externally stored or not.  This is required because for
3417	 * such attributes the flattened value won't be WAL logged as part of the
3418	 * new tuple so we must include it as part of the old_key_tuple.  See
3419	 * ExtractReplicaIdentity.
3420	 */
3421	modified_attrs = HeapDetermineColumnsInfo(relation, interesting_attrs,
3422											  id_attrs, &oldtup,
3423											  newtup, &id_has_external);
3424
3425	/*
3426	 * If we're not updating any "key" column, we can grab a weaker lock type.
3427	 * This allows for more concurrency when we are running simultaneously
3428	 * with foreign key checks.
3429	 *
3430	 * Note that if a column gets detoasted while executing the update, but
3431	 * the value ends up being the same, this test will fail and we will use
3432	 * the stronger lock.  This is acceptable; the important case to optimize
3433	 * is updates that don't manipulate key columns, not those that
3434	 * serendipitously arrive at the same key values.
3435	 */
3436	if (!bms_overlap(modified_attrs, key_attrs))
3437	{
3438		*lockmode = LockTupleNoKeyExclusive;
3439		mxact_status = MultiXactStatusNoKeyUpdate;
3440		key_intact = true;
3441
3442		/*
3443		 * If this is the first possibly-multixact-able operation in the
3444		 * current transaction, set my per-backend OldestMemberMXactId
3445		 * setting. We can be certain that the transaction will never become a
3446		 * member of any older MultiXactIds than that.  (We have to do this
3447		 * even if we end up just using our own TransactionId below, since
3448		 * some other backend could incorporate our XID into a MultiXact
3449		 * immediately afterwards.)
3450		 */
3451		MultiXactIdSetOldestMember();
3452	}
3453	else
3454	{
3455		*lockmode = LockTupleExclusive;
3456		mxact_status = MultiXactStatusUpdate;
3457		key_intact = false;
3458	}
3459
3460	/*
3461	 * Note: beyond this point, use oldtup not otid to refer to old tuple.
3462	 * otid may very well point at newtup->t_self, which we will overwrite
3463	 * with the new tuple's location, so there's great risk of confusion if we
3464	 * use otid anymore.
3465	 */
3466
3467l2:
3468	checked_lockers = false;
3469	locker_remains = false;
3470	result = HeapTupleSatisfiesUpdate(&oldtup, cid, buffer);
3471
3472	/* see below about the "no wait" case */
3473	Assert(result != TM_BeingModified || wait);
3474
3475	if (result == TM_Invisible)
3476	{
3477		UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
3478		ereport(ERROR,
3479				(errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_NOT_IN_PREREQUISITE_STATE),
3480				 errmsg("attempted to update invisible tuple")));
3481	}
3482	else if (result == TM_BeingModified && wait)
3483	{
3484		TransactionId xwait;
3485		uint16		infomask;
3486		bool		can_continue = false;
3487
3488		/*
3489		 * XXX note that we don't consider the "no wait" case here.  This
3490		 * isn't a problem currently because no caller uses that case, but it
3491		 * should be fixed if such a caller is introduced.  It wasn't a
3492		 * problem previously because this code would always wait, but now
3493		 * that some tuple locks do not conflict with one of the lock modes we
3494		 * use, it is possible that this case is interesting to handle
3495		 * specially.
3496		 *
3497		 * This may cause failures with third-party code that calls
3498		 * heap_update directly.
3499		 */
3500
3501		/* must copy state data before unlocking buffer */
3502		xwait = HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(oldtup.t_data);
3503		infomask = oldtup.t_data->t_infomask;
3504
3505		/*
3506		 * Now we have to do something about the existing locker.  If it's a
3507		 * multi, sleep on it; we might be awakened before it is completely
3508		 * gone (or even not sleep at all in some cases); we need to preserve
3509		 * it as locker, unless it is gone completely.
3510		 *
3511		 * If it's not a multi, we need to check for sleeping conditions
3512		 * before actually going to sleep.  If the update doesn't conflict
3513		 * with the locks, we just continue without sleeping (but making sure
3514		 * it is preserved).
3515		 *
3516		 * Before sleeping, we need to acquire tuple lock to establish our
3517		 * priority for the tuple (see heap_lock_tuple).  LockTuple will
3518		 * release us when we are next-in-line for the tuple.  Note we must
3519		 * not acquire the tuple lock until we're sure we're going to sleep;
3520		 * otherwise we're open for race conditions with other transactions
3521		 * holding the tuple lock which sleep on us.
3522		 *
3523		 * If we are forced to "start over" below, we keep the tuple lock;
3524		 * this arranges that we stay at the head of the line while rechecking
3525		 * tuple state.
3526		 */
3527		if (infomask & HEAP_XMAX_IS_MULTI)
3528		{
3529			TransactionId update_xact;
3530			int			remain;
3531			bool		current_is_member = false;
3532
3533			if (DoesMultiXactIdConflict((MultiXactId) xwait, infomask,
3534										*lockmode, &current_is_member))
3535			{
3536				LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
3537
3538				/*
3539				 * Acquire the lock, if necessary (but skip it when we're
3540				 * requesting a lock and already have one; avoids deadlock).
3541				 */
3542				if (!current_is_member)
3543					heap_acquire_tuplock(relation, &(oldtup.t_self), *lockmode,
3544										 LockWaitBlock, &have_tuple_lock);
3545
3546				/* wait for multixact */
3547				MultiXactIdWait((MultiXactId) xwait, mxact_status, infomask,
3548								relation, &oldtup.t_self, XLTW_Update,
3549								&remain);
3550				checked_lockers = true;
3551				locker_remains = remain != 0;
3552				LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
3553
3554				/*
3555				 * If xwait had just locked the tuple then some other xact
3556				 * could update this tuple before we get to this point.  Check
3557				 * for xmax change, and start over if so.
3558				 */
3559				if (xmax_infomask_changed(oldtup.t_data->t_infomask,
3560										  infomask) ||
3561					!TransactionIdEquals(HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(oldtup.t_data),
3562										 xwait))
3563					goto l2;
3564			}
3565
3566			/*
3567			 * Note that the multixact may not be done by now.  It could have
3568			 * surviving members; our own xact or other subxacts of this
3569			 * backend, and also any other concurrent transaction that locked
3570			 * the tuple with LockTupleKeyShare if we only got
3571			 * LockTupleNoKeyExclusive.  If this is the case, we have to be
3572			 * careful to mark the updated tuple with the surviving members in
3573			 * Xmax.
3574			 *
3575			 * Note that there could have been another update in the
3576			 * MultiXact. In that case, we need to check whether it committed
3577			 * or aborted. If it aborted we are safe to update it again;
3578			 * otherwise there is an update conflict, and we have to return
3579			 * TableTuple{Deleted, Updated} below.
3580			 *
3581			 * In the LockTupleExclusive case, we still need to preserve the
3582			 * surviving members: those would include the tuple locks we had
3583			 * before this one, which are important to keep in case this
3584			 * subxact aborts.
3585			 */
3586			if (!HEAP_XMAX_IS_LOCKED_ONLY(oldtup.t_data->t_infomask))
3587				update_xact = HeapTupleGetUpdateXid(oldtup.t_data);
3588			else
3589				update_xact = InvalidTransactionId;
3590
3591			/*
3592			 * There was no UPDATE in the MultiXact; or it aborted. No
3593			 * TransactionIdIsInProgress() call needed here, since we called
3594			 * MultiXactIdWait() above.
3595			 */
3596			if (!TransactionIdIsValid(update_xact) ||
3597				TransactionIdDidAbort(update_xact))
3598				can_continue = true;
3599		}
3600		else if (TransactionIdIsCurrentTransactionId(xwait))
3601		{
3602			/*
3603			 * The only locker is ourselves; we can avoid grabbing the tuple
3604			 * lock here, but must preserve our locking information.
3605			 */
3606			checked_lockers = true;
3607			locker_remains = true;
3608			can_continue = true;
3609		}
3610		else if (HEAP_XMAX_IS_KEYSHR_LOCKED(infomask) && key_intact)
3611		{
3612			/*
3613			 * If it's just a key-share locker, and we're not changing the key
3614			 * columns, we don't need to wait for it to end; but we need to
3615			 * preserve it as locker.
3616			 */
3617			checked_lockers = true;
3618			locker_remains = true;
3619			can_continue = true;
3620		}
3621		else
3622		{
3623			/*
3624			 * Wait for regular transaction to end; but first, acquire tuple
3625			 * lock.
3626			 */
3627			LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
3628			heap_acquire_tuplock(relation, &(oldtup.t_self), *lockmode,
3629								 LockWaitBlock, &have_tuple_lock);
3630			XactLockTableWait(xwait, relation, &oldtup.t_self,
3631							  XLTW_Update);
3632			checked_lockers = true;
3633			LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
3634
3635			/*
3636			 * xwait is done, but if xwait had just locked the tuple then some
3637			 * other xact could update this tuple before we get to this point.
3638			 * Check for xmax change, and start over if so.
3639			 */
3640			if (xmax_infomask_changed(oldtup.t_data->t_infomask, infomask) ||
3641				!TransactionIdEquals(xwait,
3642									 HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(oldtup.t_data)))
3643				goto l2;
3644
3645			/* Otherwise check if it committed or aborted */
3646			UpdateXmaxHintBits(oldtup.t_data, buffer, xwait);
3647			if (oldtup.t_data->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_INVALID)
3648				can_continue = true;
3649		}
3650
3651		if (can_continue)
3652			result = TM_Ok;
3653		else if (!ItemPointerEquals(&oldtup.t_self, &oldtup.t_data->t_ctid))
3654			result = TM_Updated;
3655		else
3656			result = TM_Deleted;
3657	}
3658
3659	/* Sanity check the result HeapTupleSatisfiesUpdate() and the logic above */
3660	if (result != TM_Ok)
3661	{
3662		Assert(result == TM_SelfModified ||
3663			   result == TM_Updated ||
3664			   result == TM_Deleted ||
3665			   result == TM_BeingModified);
3666		Assert(!(oldtup.t_data->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_INVALID));
3667		Assert(result != TM_Updated ||
3668			   !ItemPointerEquals(&oldtup.t_self, &oldtup.t_data->t_ctid));
3669	}
3670
3671	if (crosscheck != InvalidSnapshot && result == TM_Ok)
3672	{
3673		/* Perform additional check for transaction-snapshot mode RI updates */
3674		if (!HeapTupleSatisfiesVisibility(&oldtup, crosscheck, buffer))
3675			result = TM_Updated;
3676	}
3677
3678	if (result != TM_Ok)
3679	{
3680		tmfd->ctid = oldtup.t_data->t_ctid;
3681		tmfd->xmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetUpdateXid(oldtup.t_data);
3682		if (result == TM_SelfModified)
3683			tmfd->cmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetCmax(oldtup.t_data);
3684		else
3685			tmfd->cmax = InvalidCommandId;
3686		UnlockReleaseBuffer(buffer);
3687		if (have_tuple_lock)
3688			UnlockTupleTuplock(relation, &(oldtup.t_self), *lockmode);
3689		if (vmbuffer != InvalidBuffer)
3690			ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
3691		*update_indexes = TU_None;
3692
3693		bms_free(hot_attrs);
3694		bms_free(sum_attrs);
3695		bms_free(key_attrs);
3696		bms_free(id_attrs);
3697		bms_free(modified_attrs);
3698		bms_free(interesting_attrs);
3699		return result;
3700	}
3701
3702	/*
3703	 * If we didn't pin the visibility map page and the page has become all
3704	 * visible while we were busy locking the buffer, or during some
3705	 * subsequent window during which we had it unlocked, we'll have to unlock
3706	 * and re-lock, to avoid holding the buffer lock across an I/O.  That's a
3707	 * bit unfortunate, especially since we'll now have to recheck whether the
3708	 * tuple has been locked or updated under us, but hopefully it won't
3709	 * happen very often.
3710	 */
3711	if (vmbuffer == InvalidBuffer && PageIsAllVisible(page))
3712	{
3713		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
3714		visibilitymap_pin(relation, block, &vmbuffer);
3715		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
3716		goto l2;
3717	}
3718
3719	/* Fill in transaction status data */
3720
3721	/*
3722	 * If the tuple we're updating is locked, we need to preserve the locking
3723	 * info in the old tuple's Xmax.  Prepare a new Xmax value for this.
3724	 */
3725	compute_new_xmax_infomask(HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(oldtup.t_data),
3726							  oldtup.t_data->t_infomask,
3727							  oldtup.t_data->t_infomask2,
3728							  xid, *lockmode, true,
3729							  &xmax_old_tuple, &infomask_old_tuple,
3730							  &infomask2_old_tuple);
3731
3732	/*
3733	 * And also prepare an Xmax value for the new copy of the tuple.  If there
3734	 * was no xmax previously, or there was one but all lockers are now gone,
3735	 * then use InvalidTransactionId; otherwise, get the xmax from the old
3736	 * tuple.  (In rare cases that might also be InvalidTransactionId and yet
3737	 * not have the HEAP_XMAX_INVALID bit set; that's fine.)
3738	 */
3739	if ((oldtup.t_data->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_INVALID) ||
3740		HEAP_LOCKED_UPGRADED(oldtup.t_data->t_infomask) ||
3741		(checked_lockers && !locker_remains))
3742		xmax_new_tuple = InvalidTransactionId;
3743	else
3744		xmax_new_tuple = HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(oldtup.t_data);
3745
3746	if (!TransactionIdIsValid(xmax_new_tuple))
3747	{
3748		infomask_new_tuple = HEAP_XMAX_INVALID;
3749		infomask2_new_tuple = 0;
3750	}
3751	else
3752	{
3753		/*
3754		 * If we found a valid Xmax for the new tuple, then the infomask bits
3755		 * to use on the new tuple depend on what was there on the old one.
3756		 * Note that since we're doing an update, the only possibility is that
3757		 * the lockers had FOR KEY SHARE lock.
3758		 */
3759		if (oldtup.t_data->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_IS_MULTI)
3760		{
3761			GetMultiXactIdHintBits(xmax_new_tuple, &infomask_new_tuple,
3762								   &infomask2_new_tuple);
3763		}
3764		else
3765		{
3766			infomask_new_tuple = HEAP_XMAX_KEYSHR_LOCK | HEAP_XMAX_LOCK_ONLY;
3767			infomask2_new_tuple = 0;
3768		}
3769	}
3770
3771	/*
3772	 * Prepare the new tuple with the appropriate initial values of Xmin and
3773	 * Xmax, as well as initial infomask bits as computed above.
3774	 */
3775	newtup->t_data->t_infomask &= ~(HEAP_XACT_MASK);
3776	newtup->t_data->t_infomask2 &= ~(HEAP2_XACT_MASK);
3777	HeapTupleHeaderSetXmin(newtup->t_data, xid);
3778	HeapTupleHeaderSetCmin(newtup->t_data, cid);
3779	newtup->t_data->t_infomask |= HEAP_UPDATED | infomask_new_tuple;
3780	newtup->t_data->t_infomask2 |= infomask2_new_tuple;
3781	HeapTupleHeaderSetXmax(newtup->t_data, xmax_new_tuple);
3782
3783	/*
3784	 * Replace cid with a combo CID if necessary.  Note that we already put
3785	 * the plain cid into the new tuple.
3786	 */
3787	HeapTupleHeaderAdjustCmax(oldtup.t_data, &cid, &iscombo);
3788
3789	/*
3790	 * If the toaster needs to be activated, OR if the new tuple will not fit
3791	 * on the same page as the old, then we need to release the content lock
3792	 * (but not the pin!) on the old tuple's buffer while we are off doing
3793	 * TOAST and/or table-file-extension work.  We must mark the old tuple to
3794	 * show that it's locked, else other processes may try to update it
3795	 * themselves.
3796	 *
3797	 * We need to invoke the toaster if there are already any out-of-line
3798	 * toasted values present, or if the new tuple is over-threshold.
3799	 */
3800	if (relation->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
3801		relation->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_MATVIEW)
3802	{
3803		/* toast table entries should never be recursively toasted */
3804		Assert(!HeapTupleHasExternal(&oldtup));
3805		Assert(!HeapTupleHasExternal(newtup));
3806		need_toast = false;
3807	}
3808	else
3809		need_toast = (HeapTupleHasExternal(&oldtup) ||
3810					  HeapTupleHasExternal(newtup) ||
3811					  newtup->t_len > TOAST_TUPLE_THRESHOLD);
3812
3813	pagefree = PageGetHeapFreeSpace(page);
3814
3815	newtupsize = MAXALIGN(newtup->t_len);
3816
3817	if (need_toast || newtupsize > pagefree)
3818	{
3819		TransactionId xmax_lock_old_tuple;
3820		uint16		infomask_lock_old_tuple,
3821					infomask2_lock_old_tuple;
3822		bool		cleared_all_frozen = false;
3823
3824		/*
3825		 * To prevent concurrent sessions from updating the tuple, we have to
3826		 * temporarily mark it locked, while we release the page-level lock.
3827		 *
3828		 * To satisfy the rule that any xid potentially appearing in a buffer
3829		 * written out to disk, we unfortunately have to WAL log this
3830		 * temporary modification.  We can reuse xl_heap_lock for this
3831		 * purpose.  If we crash/error before following through with the
3832		 * actual update, xmax will be of an aborted transaction, allowing
3833		 * other sessions to proceed.
3834		 */
3835
3836		/*
3837		 * Compute xmax / infomask appropriate for locking the tuple. This has
3838		 * to be done separately from the combo that's going to be used for
3839		 * updating, because the potentially created multixact would otherwise
3840		 * be wrong.
3841		 */
3842		compute_new_xmax_infomask(HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(oldtup.t_data),
3843								  oldtup.t_data->t_infomask,
3844								  oldtup.t_data->t_infomask2,
3845								  xid, *lockmode, false,
3846								  &xmax_lock_old_tuple, &infomask_lock_old_tuple,
3847								  &infomask2_lock_old_tuple);
3848
3849		Assert(HEAP_XMAX_IS_LOCKED_ONLY(infomask_lock_old_tuple));
3850
3851		START_CRIT_SECTION();
3852
3853		/* Clear obsolete visibility flags ... */
3854		oldtup.t_data->t_infomask &= ~(HEAP_XMAX_BITS | HEAP_MOVED);
3855		oldtup.t_data->t_infomask2 &= ~HEAP_KEYS_UPDATED;
3856		HeapTupleClearHotUpdated(&oldtup);
3857		/* ... and store info about transaction updating this tuple */
3858		Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(xmax_lock_old_tuple));
3859		HeapTupleHeaderSetXmax(oldtup.t_data, xmax_lock_old_tuple);
3860		oldtup.t_data->t_infomask |= infomask_lock_old_tuple;
3861		oldtup.t_data->t_infomask2 |= infomask2_lock_old_tuple;
3862		HeapTupleHeaderSetCmax(oldtup.t_data, cid, iscombo);
3863
3864		/* temporarily make it look not-updated, but locked */
3865		oldtup.t_data->t_ctid = oldtup.t_self;
3866
3867		/*
3868		 * Clear all-frozen bit on visibility map if needed. We could
3869		 * immediately reset ALL_VISIBLE, but given that the WAL logging
3870		 * overhead would be unchanged, that doesn't seem necessarily
3871		 * worthwhile.
3872		 */
3873		if (PageIsAllVisible(page) &&
3874			visibilitymap_clear(relation, block, vmbuffer,
3875								VISIBILITYMAP_ALL_FROZEN))
3876			cleared_all_frozen = true;
3877
3878		MarkBufferDirty(buffer);
3879
3880		if (RelationNeedsWAL(relation))
3881		{
3882			xl_heap_lock xlrec;
3883			XLogRecPtr	recptr;
3884
3885			XLogBeginInsert();
3886			XLogRegisterBuffer(0, buffer, REGBUF_STANDARD);
3887
3888			xlrec.offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&oldtup.t_self);
3889			xlrec.xmax = xmax_lock_old_tuple;
3890			xlrec.infobits_set = compute_infobits(oldtup.t_data->t_infomask,
3891												  oldtup.t_data->t_infomask2);
3892			xlrec.flags =
3893				cleared_all_frozen ? XLH_LOCK_ALL_FROZEN_CLEARED : 0;
3894			XLogRegisterData(&xlrec, SizeOfHeapLock);
3895			recptr = XLogInsert(RM_HEAP_ID, XLOG_HEAP_LOCK);
3896			PageSetLSN(page, recptr);
3897		}
3898
3899		END_CRIT_SECTION();
3900
3901		LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
3902
3903		/*
3904		 * Let the toaster do its thing, if needed.
3905		 *
3906		 * Note: below this point, heaptup is the data we actually intend to
3907		 * store into the relation; newtup is the caller's original untoasted
3908		 * data.
3909		 */
3910		if (need_toast)
3911		{
3912			/* Note we always use WAL and FSM during updates */
3913			heaptup = heap_toast_insert_or_update(relation, newtup, &oldtup, 0);
3914			newtupsize = MAXALIGN(heaptup->t_len);
3915		}
3916		else
3917			heaptup = newtup;
3918
3919		/*
3920		 * Now, do we need a new page for the tuple, or not?  This is a bit
3921		 * tricky since someone else could have added tuples to the page while
3922		 * we weren't looking.  We have to recheck the available space after
3923		 * reacquiring the buffer lock.  But don't bother to do that if the
3924		 * former amount of free space is still not enough; it's unlikely
3925		 * there's more free now than before.
3926		 *
3927		 * What's more, if we need to get a new page, we will need to acquire
3928		 * buffer locks on both old and new pages.  To avoid deadlock against
3929		 * some other backend trying to get the same two locks in the other
3930		 * order, we must be consistent about the order we get the locks in.
3931		 * We use the rule "lock the lower-numbered page of the relation
3932		 * first".  To implement this, we must do RelationGetBufferForTuple
3933		 * while not holding the lock on the old page, and we must rely on it
3934		 * to get the locks on both pages in the correct order.
3935		 *
3936		 * Another consideration is that we need visibility map page pin(s) if
3937		 * we will have to clear the all-visible flag on either page.  If we
3938		 * call RelationGetBufferForTuple, we rely on it to acquire any such
3939		 * pins; but if we don't, we have to handle that here.  Hence we need
3940		 * a loop.
3941		 */
3942		for (;;)
3943		{
3944			if (newtupsize > pagefree)
3945			{
3946				/* It doesn't fit, must use RelationGetBufferForTuple. */
3947				newbuf = RelationGetBufferForTuple(relation, heaptup->t_len,
3948												   buffer, 0, NULL,
3949												   &vmbuffer_new, &vmbuffer,
3950												   0);
3951				/* We're all done. */
3952				break;
3953			}
3954			/* Acquire VM page pin if needed and we don't have it. */
3955			if (vmbuffer == InvalidBuffer && PageIsAllVisible(page))
3956				visibilitymap_pin(relation, block, &vmbuffer);
3957			/* Re-acquire the lock on the old tuple's page. */
3958			LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
3959			/* Re-check using the up-to-date free space */
3960			pagefree = PageGetHeapFreeSpace(page);
3961			if (newtupsize > pagefree ||
3962				(vmbuffer == InvalidBuffer && PageIsAllVisible(page)))
3963			{
3964				/*
3965				 * Rats, it doesn't fit anymore, or somebody just now set the
3966				 * all-visible flag.  We must now unlock and loop to avoid
3967				 * deadlock.  Fortunately, this path should seldom be taken.
3968				 */
3969				LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
3970			}
3971			else
3972			{
3973				/* We're all done. */
3974				newbuf = buffer;
3975				break;
3976			}
3977		}
3978	}
3979	else
3980	{
3981		/* No TOAST work needed, and it'll fit on same page */
3982		newbuf = buffer;
3983		heaptup = newtup;
3984	}
3985
3986	/*
3987	 * We're about to do the actual update -- check for conflict first, to
3988	 * avoid possibly having to roll back work we've just done.
3989	 *
3990	 * This is safe without a recheck as long as there is no possibility of
3991	 * another process scanning the pages between this check and the update
3992	 * being visible to the scan (i.e., exclusive buffer content lock(s) are
3993	 * continuously held from this point until the tuple update is visible).
3994	 *
3995	 * For the new tuple the only check needed is at the relation level, but
3996	 * since both tuples are in the same relation and the check for oldtup
3997	 * will include checking the relation level, there is no benefit to a
3998	 * separate check for the new tuple.
3999	 */
4000	CheckForSerializableConflictIn(relation, &oldtup.t_self,
4001								   BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer));
4002
4003	/*
4004	 * At this point newbuf and buffer are both pinned and locked, and newbuf
4005	 * has enough space for the new tuple.  If they are the same buffer, only
4006	 * one pin is held.
4007	 */
4008
4009	if (newbuf == buffer)
4010	{
4011		/*
4012		 * Since the new tuple is going into the same page, we might be able
4013		 * to do a HOT update.  Check if any of the index columns have been
4014		 * changed.
4015		 */
4016		if (!bms_overlap(modified_attrs, hot_attrs))
4017		{
4018			use_hot_update = true;
4019
4020			/*
4021			 * If none of the columns that are used in hot-blocking indexes
4022			 * were updated, we can apply HOT, but we do still need to check
4023			 * if we need to update the summarizing indexes, and update those
4024			 * indexes if the columns were updated, or we may fail to detect
4025			 * e.g. value bound changes in BRIN minmax indexes.
4026			 */
4027			if (bms_overlap(modified_attrs, sum_attrs))
4028				summarized_update = true;
4029		}
4030	}
4031	else
4032	{
4033		/* Set a hint that the old page could use prune/defrag */
4034		PageSetFull(page);
4035	}
4036
4037	/*
4038	 * Compute replica identity tuple before entering the critical section so
4039	 * we don't PANIC upon a memory allocation failure.
4040	 * ExtractReplicaIdentity() will return NULL if nothing needs to be
4041	 * logged.  Pass old key required as true only if the replica identity key
4042	 * columns are modified or it has external data.
4043	 */
4044	old_key_tuple = ExtractReplicaIdentity(relation, &oldtup,
4045										   bms_overlap(modified_attrs, id_attrs) ||
4046										   id_has_external,
4047										   &old_key_copied);
4048
4049	/* NO EREPORT(ERROR) from here till changes are logged */
4050	START_CRIT_SECTION();
4051
4052	/*
4053	 * If this transaction commits, the old tuple will become DEAD sooner or
4054	 * later.  Set flag that this page is a candidate for pruning once our xid
4055	 * falls below the OldestXmin horizon.  If the transaction finally aborts,
4056	 * the subsequent page pruning will be a no-op and the hint will be
4057	 * cleared.
4058	 *
4059	 * XXX Should we set hint on newbuf as well?  If the transaction aborts,
4060	 * there would be a prunable tuple in the newbuf; but for now we choose
4061	 * not to optimize for aborts.  Note that heap_xlog_update must be kept in
4062	 * sync if this decision changes.
4063	 */
4064	PageSetPrunable(page, xid);
4065
4066	if (use_hot_update)
4067	{
4068		/* Mark the old tuple as HOT-updated */
4069		HeapTupleSetHotUpdated(&oldtup);
4070		/* And mark the new tuple as heap-only */
4071		HeapTupleSetHeapOnly(heaptup);
4072		/* Mark the caller's copy too, in case different from heaptup */
4073		HeapTupleSetHeapOnly(newtup);
4074	}
4075	else
4076	{
4077		/* Make sure tuples are correctly marked as not-HOT */
4078		HeapTupleClearHotUpdated(&oldtup);
4079		HeapTupleClearHeapOnly(heaptup);
4080		HeapTupleClearHeapOnly(newtup);
4081	}
4082
4083	RelationPutHeapTuple(relation, newbuf, heaptup, false); /* insert new tuple */
4084
4085
4086	/* Clear obsolete visibility flags, possibly set by ourselves above... */
4087	oldtup.t_data->t_infomask &= ~(HEAP_XMAX_BITS | HEAP_MOVED);
4088	oldtup.t_data->t_infomask2 &= ~HEAP_KEYS_UPDATED;
4089	/* ... and store info about transaction updating this tuple */
4090	Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(xmax_old_tuple));
4091	HeapTupleHeaderSetXmax(oldtup.t_data, xmax_old_tuple);
4092	oldtup.t_data->t_infomask |= infomask_old_tuple;
4093	oldtup.t_data->t_infomask2 |= infomask2_old_tuple;
4094	HeapTupleHeaderSetCmax(oldtup.t_data, cid, iscombo);
4095
4096	/* record address of new tuple in t_ctid of old one */
4097	oldtup.t_data->t_ctid = heaptup->t_self;
4098
4099	/* clear PD_ALL_VISIBLE flags, reset all visibilitymap bits */
4100	if (PageIsAllVisible(BufferGetPage(buffer)))
4101	{
4102		all_visible_cleared = true;
4103		PageClearAllVisible(BufferGetPage(buffer));
4104		visibilitymap_clear(relation, BufferGetBlockNumber(buffer),
4105							vmbuffer, VISIBILITYMAP_VALID_BITS);
4106	}
4107	if (newbuf != buffer && PageIsAllVisible(BufferGetPage(newbuf)))
4108	{
4109		all_visible_cleared_new = true;
4110		PageClearAllVisible(BufferGetPage(newbuf));
4111		visibilitymap_clear(relation, BufferGetBlockNumber(newbuf),
4112							vmbuffer_new, VISIBILITYMAP_VALID_BITS);
4113	}
4114
4115	if (newbuf != buffer)
4116		MarkBufferDirty(newbuf);
4117	MarkBufferDirty(buffer);
4118
4119	/* XLOG stuff */
4120	if (RelationNeedsWAL(relation))
4121	{
4122		XLogRecPtr	recptr;
4123
4124		/*
4125		 * For logical decoding we need combo CIDs to properly decode the
4126		 * catalog.
4127		 */
4128		if (RelationIsAccessibleInLogicalDecoding(relation))
4129		{
4130			log_heap_new_cid(relation, &oldtup);
4131			log_heap_new_cid(relation, heaptup);
4132		}
4133
4134		recptr = log_heap_update(relation, buffer,
4135								 newbuf, &oldtup, heaptup,
4136								 old_key_tuple,
4137								 all_visible_cleared,
4138								 all_visible_cleared_new);
4139		if (newbuf != buffer)
4140		{
4141			PageSetLSN(BufferGetPage(newbuf), recptr);
4142		}
4143		PageSetLSN(BufferGetPage(buffer), recptr);
4144	}
4145
4146	END_CRIT_SECTION();
4147
4148	if (newbuf != buffer)
4149		LockBuffer(newbuf, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
4150	LockBuffer(buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_UNLOCK);
4151
4152	/*
4153	 * Mark old tuple for invalidation from system caches at next command
4154	 * boundary, and mark the new tuple for invalidation in case we abort. We
4155	 * have to do this before releasing the buffer because oldtup is in the
4156	 * buffer.  (heaptup is all in local memory, but it's necessary to process
4157	 * both tuple versions in one call to inval.c so we can avoid redundant
4158	 * sinval messages.)
4159	 */
4160	CacheInvalidateHeapTuple(relation, &oldtup, heaptup);
4161
4162	/* Now we can release the buffer(s) */
4163	if (newbuf != buffer)
4164		ReleaseBuffer(newbuf);
4165	ReleaseBuffer(buffer);
4166	if (BufferIsValid(vmbuffer_new))
4167		ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer_new);
4168	if (BufferIsValid(vmbuffer))
4169		ReleaseBuffer(vmbuffer);
4170
4171	/*
4172	 * Release the lmgr tuple lock, if we had it.
4173	 */
4174	if (have_tuple_lock)
4175		UnlockTupleTuplock(relation, &(oldtup.t_self), *lockmode);
4176
4177	pgstat_count_heap_update(relation, use_hot_update, newbuf != buffer);
4178
4179	/*
4180	 * If heaptup is a private copy, release it.  Don't forget to copy t_self
4181	 * back to the caller's image, too.
4182	 */
4183	if (heaptup != newtup)
4184	{
4185		newtup->t_self = heaptup->t_self;
4186		heap_freetuple(heaptup);
4187	}
4188
4189	/*
4190	 * If it is a HOT update, the update may still need to update summarized
4191	 * indexes, lest we fail to update those summaries and get incorrect
4192	 * results (for example, minmax bounds of the block may change with this
4193	 * update).
4194	 */
4195	if (use_hot_update)
4196	{
4197		if (summarized_update)
4198			*update_indexes = TU_Summarizing;
4199		else
4200			*update_indexes = TU_None;
4201	}
4202	else
4203		*update_indexes = TU_All;
4204
4205	if (old_key_tuple != NULL && old_key_copied)
4206		heap_freetuple(old_key_tuple);
4207
4208	bms_free(hot_attrs);
4209	bms_free(sum_attrs);
4210	bms_free(key_attrs);
4211	bms_free(id_attrs);
4212	bms_free(modified_attrs);
4213	bms_free(interesting_attrs);
4214
4215	return TM_Ok;
4216}
4217
4218#ifdef USE_ASSERT_CHECKING
4219/*
4220 * Confirm adequate lock held during heap_update(), per rules from
4221 * README.tuplock section "Locking to write inplace-updated tables".
4222 */
4223static void
4224check_lock_if_inplace_updateable_rel(Relation relation,
4225									 ItemPointer otid,
4226									 HeapTuple newtup)
4227{
4228	/* LOCKTAG_TUPLE acceptable for any catalog */
4229	switch (RelationGetRelid(relation))
4230	{
4231		case RelationRelationId:
4232		case DatabaseRelationId:
4233			{
4234				LOCKTAG		tuptag;
4235
4236				SET_LOCKTAG_TUPLE(tuptag,
4237								  relation->rd_lockInfo.lockRelId.dbId,
4238								  relation->rd_lockInfo.lockRelId.relId,
4239								  ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(otid),
4240								  ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(otid));
4241				if (LockHeldByMe(&tuptag, InplaceUpdateTupleLock, false))
4242					return;
4243			}
4244			break;
4245		default:
4246			Assert(!IsInplaceUpdateRelation(relation));
4247			return;
4248	}
4249
4250	switch (RelationGetRelid(relation))
4251	{
4252		case RelationRelationId:
4253			{
4254				/* LOCKTAG_TUPLE or LOCKTAG_RELATION ok */
4255				Form_pg_class classForm = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(newtup);
4256				Oid			relid = classForm->oid;
4257				Oid			dbid;
4258				LOCKTAG		tag;
4259
4260				if (IsSharedRelation(relid))
4261					dbid = InvalidOid;
4262				else
4263					dbid = MyDatabaseId;
4264
4265				if (classForm->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX)
4266				{
4267					Relation	irel = index_open(relid, AccessShareLock);
4268
4269					SET_LOCKTAG_RELATION(tag, dbid, irel->rd_index->indrelid);
4270					index_close(irel, AccessShareLock);
4271				}
4272				else
4273					SET_LOCKTAG_RELATION(tag, dbid, relid);
4274
4275				if (!LockHeldByMe(&tag, ShareUpdateExclusiveLock, false) &&
4276					!LockHeldByMe(&tag, ShareRowExclusiveLock, true))
4277					elog(WARNING,
4278						 "missing lock for relation \"%s\" (OID %u, relkind %c) @ TID (%u,%u)",
4279						 NameStr(classForm->relname),
4280						 relid,
4281						 classForm->relkind,
4282						 ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(otid),
4283						 ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(otid));
4284			}
4285			break;
4286		case DatabaseRelationId:
4287			{
4288				/* LOCKTAG_TUPLE required */
4289				Form_pg_database dbForm = (Form_pg_database) GETSTRUCT(newtup);
4290
4291				elog(WARNING,
4292					 "missing lock on database \"%s\" (OID %u) @ TID (%u,%u)",
4293					 NameStr(dbForm->datname),
4294					 dbForm->oid,
4295					 ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(otid),
4296					 ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(otid));
4297			}
4298			break;
4299	}
4300}
4301
4302/*
4303 * Confirm adequate relation lock held, per rules from README.tuplock section
4304 * "Locking to write inplace-updated tables".
4305 */
4306static void
4307check_inplace_rel_lock(HeapTuple oldtup)
4308{
4309	Form_pg_class classForm = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(oldtup);
4310	Oid			relid = classForm->oid;
4311	Oid			dbid;
4312	LOCKTAG		tag;
4313
4314	if (IsSharedRelation(relid))
4315		dbid = InvalidOid;
4316	else
4317		dbid = MyDatabaseId;
4318
4319	if (classForm->relkind == RELKIND_INDEX)
4320	{
4321		Relation	irel = index_open(relid, AccessShareLock);
4322
4323		SET_LOCKTAG_RELATION(tag, dbid, irel->rd_index->indrelid);
4324		index_close(irel, AccessShareLock);
4325	}
4326	else
4327		SET_LOCKTAG_RELATION(tag, dbid, relid);
4328
4329	if (!LockHeldByMe(&tag, ShareUpdateExclusiveLock, true))
4330		elog(WARNING,
4331			 "missing lock for relation \"%s\" (OID %u, relkind %c) @ TID (%u,%u)",
4332			 NameStr(classForm->relname),
4333			 relid,
4334			 classForm->relkind,
4335			 ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(&oldtup->t_self),
4336			 ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&oldtup->t_self));
4337}
4338#endif
4339
4340/*
4341 * Check if the specified attribute's values are the same.  Subroutine for
4342 * HeapDetermineColumnsInfo.
4343 */
4344static bool
4345heap_attr_equals(TupleDesc tupdesc, int attrnum, Datum value1, Datum value2,
4346				 bool isnull1, bool isnull2)
4347{
4348	/*
4349	 * If one value is NULL and other is not, then they are certainly not
4350	 * equal
4351	 */
4352	if (isnull1 != isnull2)
4353		return false;
4354
4355	/*
4356	 * If both are NULL, they can be considered equal.
4357	 */
4358	if (isnull1)
4359		return true;
4360
4361	/*
4362	 * We do simple binary comparison of the two datums.  This may be overly
4363	 * strict because there can be multiple binary representations for the
4364	 * same logical value.  But we should be OK as long as there are no false
4365	 * positives.  Using a type-specific equality operator is messy because
4366	 * there could be multiple notions of equality in different operator
4367	 * classes; furthermore, we cannot safely invoke user-defined functions
4368	 * while holding exclusive buffer lock.
4369	 */
4370	if (attrnum <= 0)
4371	{
4372		/* The only allowed system columns are OIDs, so do this */
4373		return (DatumGetObjectId(value1) == DatumGetObjectId(value2));
4374	}
4375	else
4376	{
4377		CompactAttribute *att;
4378
4379		Assert(attrnum <= tupdesc->natts);
4380		att = TupleDescCompactAttr(tupdesc, attrnum - 1);
4381		return datumIsEqual(value1, value2, att->attbyval, att->attlen);
4382	}
4383}
4384
4385/*
4386 * Check which columns are being updated.
4387 *
4388 * Given an updated tuple, determine (and return into the output bitmapset),
4389 * from those listed as interesting, the set of columns that changed.
4390 *
4391 * has_external indicates if any of the unmodified attributes (from those
4392 * listed as interesting) of the old tuple is a member of external_cols and is
4393 * stored externally.
4394 */
4395static Bitmapset *
4396HeapDetermineColumnsInfo(Relation relation,
4397						 Bitmapset *interesting_cols,
4398						 Bitmapset *external_cols,
4399						 HeapTuple oldtup, HeapTuple newtup,
4400						 bool *has_external)
4401{
4402	int			attidx;
4403	Bitmapset  *modified = NULL;
4404	TupleDesc	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(relation);
4405
4406	attidx = -1;
4407	while ((attidx = bms_next_member(interesting_cols, attidx)) >= 0)
4408	{
4409		/* attidx is zero-based, attrnum is the normal attribute number */
4410		AttrNumber	attrnum = attidx + FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber;
4411		Datum		value1,
4412					value2;
4413		bool		isnull1,
4414					isnull2;
4415
4416		/*
4417		 * If it's a whole-tuple reference, say "not equal".  It's not really
4418		 * worth supporting this case, since it could only succeed after a
4419		 * no-op update, which is hardly a case worth optimizing for.
4420		 */
4421		if (attrnum == 0)
4422		{
4423			modified = bms_add_member(modified, attidx);
4424			continue;
4425		}
4426
4427		/*
4428		 * Likewise, automatically say "not equal" for any system attribute
4429		 * other than tableOID; we cannot expect these to be consistent in a
4430		 * HOT chain, or even to be set correctly yet in the new tuple.
4431		 */
4432		if (attrnum < 0)
4433		{
4434			if (attrnum != TableOidAttributeNumber)
4435			{
4436				modified = bms_add_member(modified, attidx);
4437				continue;
4438			}
4439		}
4440
4441		/*
4442		 * Extract the corresponding values.  XXX this is pretty inefficient
4443		 * if there are many indexed columns.  Should we do a single
4444		 * heap_deform_tuple call on each tuple, instead?	But that doesn't
4445		 * work for system columns ...
4446		 */
4447		value1 = heap_getattr(oldtup, attrnum, tupdesc, &isnull1);
4448		value2 = heap_getattr(newtup, attrnum, tupdesc, &isnull2);
4449
4450		if (!heap_attr_equals(tupdesc, attrnum, value1,
4451							  value2, isnull1, isnull2))
4452		{
4453			modified = bms_add_member(modified, attidx);
4454			continue;
4455		}
4456
4457		/*
4458		 * No need to check attributes that can't be stored externally. Note
4459		 * that system attributes can't be stored externally.
4460		 */
4461		if (attrnum < 0 || isnull1 ||
4462			TupleDescCompactAttr(tupdesc, attrnum - 1)->attlen != -1)
4463			continue;
4464
4465		/*
4466		 * Check if the old tuple's attribute is stored externally and is a
4467		 * member of external_cols.
4468		 */
4469		if (VARATT_IS_EXTERNAL((struct varlena *) DatumGetPointer(value1)) &&
4470			bms_is_member(attidx, external_cols))
4471			*has_external = true;
4472	}
4473
4474	return modified;
4475}
4476
4477/*
4478 *	simple_heap_update - replace a tuple
4479 *
4480 * This routine may be used to update a tuple when concurrent updates of
4481 * the target tuple are not expected (for example, because we have a lock
4482 * on the relation associated with the tuple).  Any failure is reported
4483 * via ereport().
4484 */
4485void
4486simple_heap_update(Relation relation, ItemPointer otid, HeapTuple tup,
4487				   TU_UpdateIndexes *update_indexes)
4488{
4489	TM_Result	result;
4490	TM_FailureData tmfd;
4491	LockTupleMode lockmode;
4492
4493	result = heap_update(relation, otid, tup,
4494						 GetCurrentCommandId(true), InvalidSnapshot,
4495						 true /* wait for commit */ ,
4496						 &tmfd, &lockmode, update_indexes);
4497	switch (result)
4498	{
4499		case TM_SelfModified:
4500			/* Tuple was already updated in current command? */
4501			elog(ERROR, "tuple already updated by self");
4502			break;
4503
4504		case TM_Ok:
4505			/* done successfully */
4506			break;
4507
4508		case TM_Updated:
4509			elog(ERROR, "tuple concurrently updated");
4510			break;
4511
4512		case TM_Deleted:
4513			elog(ERROR, "tuple concurrently deleted");
4514			break;
4515
4516		default:
4517			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized heap_update status: %u", result);
4518			break;
4519	}
4520}
4521
4522
4523/*
4524 * Return the MultiXactStatus corresponding to the given tuple lock mode.
4525 */
4526static MultiXactStatus
4527get_mxact_status_for_lock(LockTupleMode mode, bool is_update)
4528{
4529	int			retval;
4530
4531	if (is_update)
4532		retval = tupleLockExtraInfo[mode].updstatus;
4533	else
4534		retval = tupleLockExtraInfo[mode].lockstatus;
4535
4536	if (retval == -1)
4537		elog(ERROR, "invalid lock tuple mode %d/%s", mode,
4538			 is_update ? "true" : "false");
4539
4540	return (MultiXactStatus) retval;
4541}
4542
4543/*
4544 *	heap_lock_tuple - lock a tuple in shared or exclusive mode
4545 *
4546 * Note that this acquires a buffer pin, which the caller must release.
4547 *
4548 * Input parameters:
4549 *	relation: relation containing tuple (caller must hold suitable lock)
4550 *	tid: TID of tuple to lock
4551 *	cid: current command ID (used for visibility test, and stored into
4552 *		tuple's cmax if lock is successful)
4553 *	mode: indicates if shared or exclusive tuple lock is desired
4554 *	wait_policy: what to do if tuple lock is not available
4555 *	follow_updates: if true, follow the update chain to also lock descendant
4556 *		tuples.
4557 *
4558 * Output parameters:
4559 *	*tuple: all fields filled in
4560 *	*buffer: set to buffer holding tuple (pinned but not locked at exit)
4561 *	*tmfd: filled in failure cases (see below)
4562 *
4563 * Function results are the same as the ones for table_tuple_lock().
4564 *
4565 * In the failure cases other than TM_Invisible, the routine fills
4566 * *tmfd with the tuple's t_ctid, t_xmax (resolving a possible MultiXact,
4567 * if necessary), and t_cmax (the last only for TM_SelfModified,
4568 * since we cannot obtain cmax from a combo CID generated by another
4569 * transaction).
4570 * See comments for struct TM_FailureData for additional info.
4571 *
4572 * See README.tuplock for a thorough explanation of this mechanism.
4573 */
4574TM_Result
4575heap_lock_tuple(Relation relation, HeapTuple tuple,
4576				CommandId cid, LockTupleMode mode, LockWaitPolicy wait_policy,
4577				bool follow_updates,
4578				Buffer *buffer, TM_FailureData *tmfd)
4579{
4580	TM_Result	result;
4581	ItemPointer tid = &(tuple->t_self);
4582	ItemId		lp;
4583	Page		page;
4584	Buffer		vmbuffer = InvalidBuffer;
4585	BlockNumber block;
4586	TransactionId xid,
4587				xmax;
4588	uint16		old_infomask,
4589				new_infomask,
4590				new_infomask2;
4591	bool		first_time = true;
4592	bool		skip_tuple_lock = false;
4593	bool		have_tuple_lock = false;
4594	bool		cleared_all_frozen = false;
4595
4596	*buffer = ReadBuffer(relation, ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid));
4597	block = ItemPointerGetBlockNumber(tid);
4598
4599	/*
4600	 * Before locking the buffer, pin the visibility map page if it appears to
4601	 * be necessary.  Since we haven't got the lock yet, someone else might be
4602	 * in the middle of changing this, so we'll need to recheck after we have
4603	 * the lock.
4604	 */
4605	if (PageIsAllVisible(BufferGetPage(*buffer)))
4606		visibilitymap_pin(relation, block, &vmbuffer);
4607
4608	LockBuffer(*buffer, BUFFER_LOCK_EXCLUSIVE);
4609
4610	page = BufferGetPage(*buffer);
4611	lp = PageGetItemId(page, ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(tid));
4612	Assert(ItemIdIsNormal(lp));
4613
4614	tuple->t_data = (HeapTupleHeader) PageGetItem(page, lp);
4615	tuple->t_len = ItemIdGetLength(lp);
4616	tuple->t_tableOid = RelationGetRelid(relation);
4617
4618l3:
4619	result = HeapTupleSatisfiesUpdate(tuple, cid, *buffer);
4620
4621	if (result == TM_Invisible)
4622	{
4623		/*
4624		 * This is possible, but only when locking a tuple for ON CONFLICT
4625		 * UPDATE.  We return this value here rather than throwing an error in
