8936		xlrec.flags |= XLH_UPDATE_SUFFIX_FROM_OLD;
8937	if (need_tuple_data)
8938	{
8939		xlrec.flags |= XLH_UPDATE_CONTAINS_NEW_TUPLE;
8940		if (old_key_tuple)
8941		{
8942			if (reln->rd_rel->relreplident == REPLICA_IDENTITY_FULL)
8943				xlrec.flags |= XLH_UPDATE_CONTAINS_OLD_TUPLE;
8944			else
8945				xlrec.flags |= XLH_UPDATE_CONTAINS_OLD_KEY;
8946		}
8947	}
8948
8949	/* If new tuple is the single and first tuple on page... */
8950	if (ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&(newtup->t_self)) == FirstOffsetNumber &&
8951		PageGetMaxOffsetNumber(page) == FirstOffsetNumber)
8952	{
8953		info |= XLOG_HEAP_INIT_PAGE;
8954		init = true;
8955	}
8956	else
8957		init = false;
8958
8959	/* Prepare WAL data for the old page */
8960	xlrec.old_offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&oldtup->t_self);
8961	xlrec.old_xmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(oldtup->t_data);
8962	xlrec.old_infobits_set = compute_infobits(oldtup->t_data->t_infomask,
8963											  oldtup->t_data->t_infomask2);
8964
8965	/* Prepare WAL data for the new page */
8966	xlrec.new_offnum = ItemPointerGetOffsetNumber(&newtup->t_self);
8967	xlrec.new_xmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetRawXmax(newtup->t_data);
8968
8969	bufflags = REGBUF_STANDARD;
8970	if (init)
8971		bufflags |= REGBUF_WILL_INIT;
8972	if (need_tuple_data)
8973		bufflags |= REGBUF_KEEP_DATA;
8974
8975	XLogRegisterBuffer(0, newbuf, bufflags);
8976	if (oldbuf != newbuf)
8977		XLogRegisterBuffer(1, oldbuf, REGBUF_STANDARD);
8978
8979	XLogRegisterData(&xlrec, SizeOfHeapUpdate);
8980
8981	/*
8982	 * Prepare WAL data for the new tuple.
8983	 */
8984	if (prefixlen > 0 || suffixlen > 0)
8985	{
8986		if (prefixlen > 0 && suffixlen > 0)
8987		{
8988			prefix_suffix[0] = prefixlen;
8989			prefix_suffix[1] = suffixlen;
8990			XLogRegisterBufData(0, &prefix_suffix, sizeof(uint16) * 2);
8991		}
8992		else if (prefixlen > 0)
8993		{
8994			XLogRegisterBufData(0, &prefixlen, sizeof(uint16));
8995		}
8996		else
8997		{
8998			XLogRegisterBufData(0, &suffixlen, sizeof(uint16));
8999		}
9000	}
9001
9002	xlhdr.t_infomask2 = newtup->t_data->t_infomask2;
9003	xlhdr.t_infomask = newtup->t_data->t_infomask;
9004	xlhdr.t_hoff = newtup->t_data->t_hoff;
9005	Assert(SizeofHeapTupleHeader + prefixlen + suffixlen <= newtup->t_len);
9006
9007	/*
9008	 * PG73FORMAT: write bitmap [+ padding] [+ oid] + data
9009	 *
9010	 * The 'data' doesn't include the common prefix or suffix.
9011	 */
9012	XLogRegisterBufData(0, &xlhdr, SizeOfHeapHeader);
9013	if (prefixlen == 0)
9014	{
9015		XLogRegisterBufData(0,
9016							(char *) newtup->t_data + SizeofHeapTupleHeader,
9017							newtup->t_len - SizeofHeapTupleHeader - suffixlen);
9018	}
9019	else
9020	{
9021		/*
9022		 * Have to write the null bitmap and data after the common prefix as
9023		 * two separate rdata entries.
9024		 */
9025		/* bitmap [+ padding] [+ oid] */
9026		if (newtup->t_data->t_hoff - SizeofHeapTupleHeader > 0)
9027		{
9028			XLogRegisterBufData(0,
9029								(char *) newtup->t_data + SizeofHeapTupleHeader,
9030								newtup->t_data->t_hoff - SizeofHeapTupleHeader);
9031		}
9032
9033		/* data after common prefix */
9034		XLogRegisterBufData(0,
9035							(char *) newtup->t_data + newtup->t_data->t_hoff + prefixlen,
9036							newtup->t_len - newtup->t_data->t_hoff - prefixlen - suffixlen);
9037	}
9038
9039	/* We need to log a tuple identity */
9040	if (need_tuple_data && old_key_tuple)
9041	{
9042		/* don't really need this, but its more comfy to decode */
9043		xlhdr_idx.t_infomask2 = old_key_tuple->t_data->t_infomask2;
9044		xlhdr_idx.t_infomask = old_key_tuple->t_data->t_infomask;
9045		xlhdr_idx.t_hoff = old_key_tuple->t_data->t_hoff;
9046
9047		XLogRegisterData(&xlhdr_idx, SizeOfHeapHeader);
9048
9049		/* PG73FORMAT: write bitmap [+ padding] [+ oid] + data */
9050		XLogRegisterData((char *) old_key_tuple->t_data + SizeofHeapTupleHeader,
9051						 old_key_tuple->t_len - SizeofHeapTupleHeader);
9052	}
9053
9054	/* filtering by origin on a row level is much more efficient */
9055	XLogSetRecordFlags(XLOG_INCLUDE_ORIGIN);
9056
9057	recptr = XLogInsert(RM_HEAP_ID, info);
9058
9059	return recptr;
9060}
9061
9062/*
9063 * Perform XLogInsert of an XLOG_HEAP2_NEW_CID record
9064 *
9065 * This is only used in wal_level >= WAL_LEVEL_LOGICAL, and only for catalog
9066 * tuples.
9067 */
9068static XLogRecPtr
9069log_heap_new_cid(Relation relation, HeapTuple tup)
9070{
9071	xl_heap_new_cid xlrec;
9072
9073	XLogRecPtr	recptr;
9074	HeapTupleHeader hdr = tup->t_data;
9075
9076	Assert(ItemPointerIsValid(&tup->t_self));
9077	Assert(tup->t_tableOid != InvalidOid);
9078
9079	xlrec.top_xid = GetTopTransactionId();
9080	xlrec.target_locator = relation->rd_locator;
9081	xlrec.target_tid = tup->t_self;
9082
9083	/*
9084	 * If the tuple got inserted & deleted in the same TX we definitely have a
9085	 * combo CID, set cmin and cmax.
9086	 */
9087	if (hdr->t_infomask & HEAP_COMBOCID)
9088	{
9089		Assert(!(hdr->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_INVALID));
9090		Assert(!HeapTupleHeaderXminInvalid(hdr));
9091		xlrec.cmin = HeapTupleHeaderGetCmin(hdr);
9092		xlrec.cmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetCmax(hdr);
9093		xlrec.combocid = HeapTupleHeaderGetRawCommandId(hdr);
9094	}
9095	/* No combo CID, so only cmin or cmax can be set by this TX */
9096	else
9097	{
9098		/*
9099		 * Tuple inserted.
9100		 *
9101		 * We need to check for LOCK ONLY because multixacts might be
9102		 * transferred to the new tuple in case of FOR KEY SHARE updates in
9103		 * which case there will be an xmax, although the tuple just got
9104		 * inserted.
9105		 */
9106		if (hdr->t_infomask & HEAP_XMAX_INVALID ||
9107			HEAP_XMAX_IS_LOCKED_ONLY(hdr->t_infomask))
9108		{
9109			xlrec.cmin = HeapTupleHeaderGetRawCommandId(hdr);
9110			xlrec.cmax = InvalidCommandId;
9111		}
9112		/* Tuple from a different tx updated or deleted. */
9113		else
9114		{
9115			xlrec.cmin = InvalidCommandId;
9116			xlrec.cmax = HeapTupleHeaderGetRawCommandId(hdr);
9117		}
9118		xlrec.combocid = InvalidCommandId;
9119	}
9120
9121	/*
9122	 * Note that we don't need to register the buffer here, because this
9123	 * operation does not modify the page. The insert/update/delete that
9124	 * called us certainly did, but that's WAL-logged separately.
9125	 */
9126	XLogBeginInsert();
9127	XLogRegisterData(&xlrec, SizeOfHeapNewCid);
9128
9129	/* will be looked at irrespective of origin */
9130
9131	recptr = XLogInsert(RM_HEAP2_ID, XLOG_HEAP2_NEW_CID);
9132
9133	return recptr;
9134}
9135
9136/*
9137 * Build a heap tuple representing the configured REPLICA IDENTITY to represent
9138 * the old tuple in an UPDATE or DELETE.
9139 *
9140 * Returns NULL if there's no need to log an identity or if there's no suitable
9141 * key defined.
9142 *
9143 * Pass key_required true if any replica identity columns changed value, or if
9144 * any of them have any external data.  Delete must always pass true.
9145 *
9146 * *copy is set to true if the returned tuple is a modified copy rather than
9147 * the same tuple that was passed in.
9148 */
9149static HeapTuple
9150ExtractReplicaIdentity(Relation relation, HeapTuple tp, bool key_required,
9151					   bool *copy)
9152{
9153	TupleDesc	desc = RelationGetDescr(relation);
9154	char		replident = relation->rd_rel->relreplident;
9155	Bitmapset  *idattrs;
9156	HeapTuple	key_tuple;
9157	bool		nulls[MaxHeapAttributeNumber];
9158	Datum		values[MaxHeapAttributeNumber];
9159
9160	*copy = false;
9161
9162	if (!RelationIsLogicallyLogged(relation))
9163		return NULL;
9164
9165	if (replident == REPLICA_IDENTITY_NOTHING)
9166		return NULL;
9167
9168	if (replident == REPLICA_IDENTITY_FULL)
9169	{
9170		/*
9171		 * When logging the entire old tuple, it very well could contain
9172		 * toasted columns. If so, force them to be inlined.
9173		 */
9174		if (HeapTupleHasExternal(tp))
9175		{
9176			*copy = true;
9177			tp = toast_flatten_tuple(tp, desc);
9178		}
9179		return tp;
9180	}
9181
9182	/* if the key isn't required and we're only logging the key, we're done */
9183	if (!key_required)
9184		return NULL;
9185
9186	/* find out the replica identity columns */
9187	idattrs = RelationGetIndexAttrBitmap(relation,
9188										 INDEX_ATTR_BITMAP_IDENTITY_KEY);
9189
9190	/*
9191	 * If there's no defined replica identity columns, treat as !key_required.
9192	 * (This case should not be reachable from heap_update, since that should
9193	 * calculate key_required accurately.  But heap_delete just passes
9194	 * constant true for key_required, so we can hit this case in deletes.)
9195	 */
9196	if (bms_is_empty(idattrs))
9197		return NULL;
9198
9199	/*
9200	 * Construct a new tuple containing only the replica identity columns,
9201	 * with nulls elsewhere.  While we're at it, assert that the replica
9202	 * identity columns aren't null.
9203	 */
9204	heap_deform_tuple(tp, desc, values, nulls);
9205
9206	for (int i = 0; i < desc->natts; i++)
9207	{
9208		if (bms_is_member(i + 1 - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber,
9209						  idattrs))
9210			Assert(!nulls[i]);
9211		else
9212			nulls[i] = true;
9213	}
9214
9215	key_tuple = heap_form_tuple(desc, values, nulls);
9216	*copy = true;
9217
9218	bms_free(idattrs);
9219
9220	/*
9221	 * If the tuple, which by here only contains indexed columns, still has
9222	 * toasted columns, force them to be inlined. This is somewhat unlikely
9223	 * since there's limits on the size of indexed columns, so we don't
9224	 * duplicate toast_flatten_tuple()s functionality in the above loop over
9225	 * the indexed columns, even if it would be more efficient.
9226	 */
9227	if (HeapTupleHasExternal(key_tuple))
9228	{
9229		HeapTuple	oldtup = key_tuple;
9230
9231		key_tuple = toast_flatten_tuple(oldtup, desc);
9232		heap_freetuple(oldtup);
9233	}
9234
9235	return key_tuple;
9236}
9237
9238/*
9239 * HeapCheckForSerializableConflictOut
9240 *		We are reading a tuple.  If it's not visible, there may be a
9241 *		rw-conflict out with the inserter.  Otherwise, if it is visible to us
9242 *		but has been deleted, there may be a rw-conflict out with the deleter.
9243 *
9244 * We will determine the top level xid of the writing transaction with which
9245 * we may be in conflict, and ask CheckForSerializableConflictOut() to check
9246 * for overlap with our own transaction.
9247 *
9248 * This function should be called just about anywhere in heapam.c where a
9249 * tuple has been read. The caller must hold at least a shared lock on the
9250 * buffer, because this function might set hint bits on the tuple. There is
9251 * currently no known reason to call this function from an index AM.
9252 */
9253void
9254HeapCheckForSerializableConflictOut(bool visible, Relation relation,
9255									HeapTuple tuple, Buffer buffer,
9256									Snapshot snapshot)
9257{
9258	TransactionId xid;
9259	HTSV_Result htsvResult;
9260
9261	if (!CheckForSerializableConflictOutNeeded(relation, snapshot))
9262		return;
9263
9264	/*
9265	 * Check to see whether the tuple has been written to by a concurrent
9266	 * transaction, either to create it not visible to us, or to delete it
9267	 * while it is visible to us.  The "visible" bool indicates whether the
9268	 * tuple is visible to us, while HeapTupleSatisfiesVacuum checks what else
9269	 * is going on with it.
9270	 *
9271	 * In the event of a concurrently inserted tuple that also happens to have
9272	 * been concurrently updated (by a separate transaction), the xmin of the
9273	 * tuple will be used -- not the updater's xid.
9274	 */
9275	htsvResult = HeapTupleSatisfiesVacuum(tuple, TransactionXmin, buffer);
9276	switch (htsvResult)
9277	{
9278		case HEAPTUPLE_LIVE:
9279			if (visible)
9280				return;
9281			xid = HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(tuple->t_data);
9282			break;
9283		case HEAPTUPLE_RECENTLY_DEAD:
9284		case HEAPTUPLE_DELETE_IN_PROGRESS:
9285			if (visible)
9286				xid = HeapTupleHeaderGetUpdateXid(tuple->t_data);
9287			else
9288				xid = HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(tuple->t_data);
9289
9290			if (TransactionIdPrecedes(xid, TransactionXmin))
9291			{
9292				/* This is like the HEAPTUPLE_DEAD case */
9293				Assert(!visible);
9294				return;
9295			}
9296			break;
9297		case HEAPTUPLE_INSERT_IN_PROGRESS:
9298			xid = HeapTupleHeaderGetXmin(tuple->t_data);
9299			break;
9300		case HEAPTUPLE_DEAD:
9301			Assert(!visible);
9302			return;
9303		default:
9304
9305			/*
9306			 * The only way to get to this default clause is if a new value is
9307			 * added to the enum type without adding it to this switch
9308			 * statement.  That's a bug, so elog.
9309			 */
9310			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized return value from HeapTupleSatisfiesVacuum: %u", htsvResult);
9311
9312			/*
9313			 * In spite of having all enum values covered and calling elog on
9314			 * this default, some compilers think this is a code path which
9315			 * allows xid to be used below without initialization. Silence
9316			 * that warning.
9317			 */
9318			xid = InvalidTransactionId;
9319	}
9320
9321	Assert(TransactionIdIsValid(xid));
9322	Assert(TransactionIdFollowsOrEquals(xid, TransactionXmin));
9323
9324	/*
9325	 * Find top level xid.  Bail out if xid is too early to be a conflict, or
9326	 * if it's our own xid.
9327	 */
9328	if (TransactionIdEquals(xid, GetTopTransactionIdIfAny()))
9329		return;
9330	xid = SubTransGetTopmostTransaction(xid);
9331	if (TransactionIdPrecedes(xid, TransactionXmin))
9332		return;
9333
9334	CheckForSerializableConflictOut(relation, xid, snapshot);
9335}
